Adiutor.name: Adiutor
Adiutor.publicNotes:
Adiutors, while nicknamed auxiliary devils, are not truly devils, but mortals invested with infernal power. These mortals gain a taste of Hell's might as rewards for their loyalty to the cause of tyranny and oppression. Hungry for power, an adiutor ruthlessly enforces Hell's will at any cost in hopes of fulfilling their fiendish ambitions. They're most commonly found in Cheliax and its territories due to the nation's relationship with the forces of Hell, serving as spies and informants for both the Thrunes and powerful devils invested in meddling with mortals.

While some adiutors climb the ranks of mortal society to become Hellknights and other supporters of tyranny, most have higher ambitions and desire to become devils themselves. To this end, they seek to serve more powerful devils and make deals that will benefit them so that they can ensure their transformation. There's nothing an adiutor despises more than their own mortality—a sign of weakness, when beings like devils walk the Universe in their perfect Hellish vision.

Although they're most likely to be found in places or near people with existing links to Hell, adiutors can be found anywhere, quietly plotting to gain power and status so they can become the lieutenants of Hell they desire to be.

weapon->Flintlock Musket.name: Flintlock Musket
weapon->Flintlock Musket.description: The most commonly available of firearms in Alkenstar, the flintlock musket includes an external firing mechanism and an efficient and relatively compact frame. Though lacking the range and firing power of the arquebus preferred by Alkenstar and Dongun Hold's military members, the flintlock musket is popular among civilians for its ease of use.

armor->Uniform.name: Uniform
armor->Uniform.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

action->Avernian Armskote.name: Avernian Armskote
action->Avernian Armskote.description: An adiutor reloads by magically conjuring hellfire rounds crafted in the Fools' Citadels of Avernus. Their Strikes with their flintlock muskets are magical and deal an additional 2d6 fire damage.

action->Fiendish Reload.name: Fiendish Reload
action->Fiendish Reload.description: When the adiutor reloads their musket, they can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.TVKNbcgTee19PXZR]{Shield} as part of the same action.

action->Punish the Wayward.name: Punish the Wayward
action->Punish the Wayward.description:
The adiutor makes a melee or ranged Strike. If this Strike succeeds, the targeted creature takes the normal damage and must additionally attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save.

Critical Success The creature suffers no additional effects.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is frightened 1. While it can see the adiutor, it can't reduce its frightened condition.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the adiutor while frightened.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Halberd.name: Halberd
weapon->Halberd.description: This polearm has a relatively short, 5-foot shaft. The business end is a long spike with an axe blade attached.

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Pinning Strike.name: Pinning Strike
action->Pinning Strike.description: The adjutant Hellknight armiger makes a halberd Strike. On a success, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until the beginning of the adjutant's next turn, until the adjutant moves more than 10 feet away from the target, or the adjutant makes another halberd Strike, whichever comes first. The target can also attempt to [[/act escape dc=18]].

Alceo Demetrias.name: Alceo Demetrias
Alceo Demetrias.blurb: Male aiuvarin human cleric of Milani

weapon->Morningstar.name: Morningstar
weapon->Morningstar.description: This weapon has a short shaft ending in a metal ball studded with spikes.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

equipment->Religious Symbol (Milani).name: Religious Symbol (Milani)
equipment->Religious Symbol (Milani).description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->Defy Devils!.name: Defy Devils!
action->Defy Devils!.description:
Trigger A creature within 30 feet critically fails a saving throw or skill check against a devil, or a creature that worships devils or has a magical contract with a devil

Effect The triggering creature rerolls the check and must take the new result. It then becomes temporarily immune to Defy Devils! for 24 hours.

action->Divine Weapon of Milani.name: Divine Weapon of Milani
action->Divine Weapon of Milani.description:
Frequency once per turn

Trigger Alceo finishes Casting a Spell using one of his spell slots on his turn

Effect Milani blesses Alceo's morningstar. Until the end of his turn, his morningstar Strikes deal an additional @Damage[2d4[spirit]] damage or @Damage[2d6[spirit]] damage if the target is unholy.

action->Sense Heartbond.name: Sense Heartbond
action->Sense Heartbond.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Alceo learns Phoebe's present state. He knows her direction and distance, and any conditions affecting her.

Aurocan.name: Aurocan
Aurocan.publicNotes:
One can predict what foes are on the battlefield by how they sound. Foot soldiers clang weapons on shields as they march. Cavalry is marked by a rapid drumbeat of hooves. Armies with great monsters under their command don't try to silence their hair-raising screeches.

No sound prompts as much caution as the steady clicking of gears and hiss of billowing steam, for the only thing that accompanies that chorus is an aurocan: a mechanical creature created in the shape of a massive six-legged bull prone to exploding when destroyed. The aurocan's greatest strength is how its head can detach from its body to rain down scalding steam from above.

action->Wind-Up.name: Wind-Up
action->Wind-Up.description:
24 hours, [[/act disable-device dc=24]], standby

For a clockwork to act, it must be wound with a unique key by another creature. This takes 1 minute. Once wound, it remains operational for the listed amount of time, usually 24 hours, after which time it becomes unaware of its surroundings and can't act until it's wound again. Some clockworks' abilities require them to spend some of their remaining operational time. They can't spend more than they have and shut down immediately once they have 0 time remaining. If it's unclear when a clockwork was last wound, most clockwork keepers wind all their clockworks at a set time, typically 8 a.m.

A clockwork that lists standby in its wind-up entry can enter standby mode as a 3-action activity. Its operational time doesn't decrease in standby, but it can sense its surroundings (with a -2 penalty to Perception). It can't act, with one exception: when it perceives a creature, it can exit standby as a reaction (rolling initiative if appropriate).

A creature can attempt to Disable a Device to wind a clockwork down (with a DC listed in the wind-up entry). For each success, the clockwork loses 1 hour of operational time. This can be done even if the clockwork is in standby mode.

action->Explosive End.name: Explosive End
action->Explosive End.description:
Trigger The aurocan drops to 0 HP

Effect The aurocan detonates. Every creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] takes @Damage[3d12[bludgeoning],3d8[fire]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:23|basic] save). On a critical failure, a creature is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

action->Blistering Touch.name: Blistering Touch
action->Blistering Touch.description: The aurocan funnels some of the steam powering it into its body, heating up its metal frame. For the next minute, its melee Strikes deal an additional 1d6 fire damage.

action->Head of Steam.name: Head of Steam
action->Head of Steam.description:
Requirements The aurocan has used Blistering Touch within the past 2 rounds, and its head is attached

Effect The aurocan expels a cloud of steam as its head detaches. The head occupies a 5-foot square and has a fly Speed of 15 feet. As part of this action, and every time the aurocan Sustains it on a subsequent round, the head can Fly up to its Speed, blasting steam directly downward as it moves. Each creature whose space it passes no more than 5 feet above during its flight takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save). On a failed save, a creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} by the steam for 1 round (or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} on a critical failure).

While its head is detached, the aurocan can't make horn Strikes, but it can perform all other actions normally. The head has an AC of 27, Hardness 10, and the same saving throws, immunities, weaknesses, and resistances as the aurocan. If the head takes 20 damage in a single attack, it falls to the ground broken, and the aurocan is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round. If the head reenters the aurocan's space, it reattaches.

Besunas.name: Besunas
Besunas.publicNotes: Wherever learning and instruction is called for within the burning shadows of Hell, from the bureaus of Dis and coffers of Erebus to the war colleges of Malebolge and Caina's courtrooms, docent devils ply their unassuming and perspicacious trade. The instillo, so named for their creativity and talent in instilling a respect for Hell's ways and legitimacy to captive audiences, are valued for their ability to transform recently conquered lands into compliant, even loyal, client-states. The conquered are taught to be ashamed of their origins; the despairing are issued philosophies that displace blame and uncertainty upon the mistakes of history and geography; the orphaned are taught to respect those who orphaned them.
Besunas.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

weapon->Light Mace.name: Light Mace
weapon->Light Mace.description: A light mace has a short wooden or metal shaft ending with a dense metal head. Used much like a club, it delivers heavy bludgeoning blows, but with extra power derived from the head's metal ridges or spikes.

equipment->Clothing (Fine).name: Clothing (Fine)
equipment->Clothing (Fine).description: Fine clothing, suitable for a noble or royal, is made with expensive fabrics, precious metals, and intricate patterns.

equipment->Writing Set.name: Writing Set
equipment->Writing Set.description: Using a writing set, you can draft correspondence and scribe scrolls. A set includes stationery, including a variety of paper and parchment, as well as ink, a quill or inkpen, sealing wax, and a simple seal. If you've written a large amount, you can refill your kit with extra ink and paper.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Correcting Veil.name: Correcting Veil
action->Correcting Veil.description: 100 feet. The instillo's telepathic presence constantly monitors perspectives and molds thought toward an infernal clarity. Creatures within the aura gain the Dubious Knowledge skill feat but are unaware of this effect. The instillo is instantly aware when any creature within the aura fails a Recall Knowledge check and influences the erroneous information that the creature gains.

action->Nod of the Tongue.name: Nod of the Tongue
action->Nod of the Tongue.description: Creatures who can understand Diabolic take a –2 status penalty to their Will saves against the instillo's effects with the auditory or mental trait.

action->Quiet Confidence.name: Quiet Confidence
action->Quiet Confidence.description: The instillo can hide the source of their magic. If the next action the instillo uses is to Cast a Spell, that spell gains the subtle trait. The trait hides only the spell's spellcasting actions and manifestations, not its effects.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The instillo's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Superlative Rhetoric.name: Superlative Rhetoric
action->Superlative Rhetoric.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The instillo's most recent action was to cast a spell with the auditory trait

Effect The instillo immediately casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4gBIw4IDrSfFHik4]{Daze} at a creature that can hear them.

Blinding Spray.name: Blinding Spray
Blinding Spray.descriptionHazard: A bladder of powdered peppers is secreted in a compartment above the door.
Blinding Spray.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:20] (trained) to disable the blade that bursts the bladder

action->No Orphans Allowed.name: No Orphans Allowed
action->No Orphans Allowed.description:
Trigger The door is opened

Effect The powder sprays in a burst that fills the square in front of the door and all adjacent squares in the orphans' dormitory. Any creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round.

Critical Failure The creature is blinded for 1 minute.

weapon->Hand Crossbow.name: Hand Crossbow
weapon->Hand Crossbow.description: Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It's small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.

weapon->Light Mace.name: Light Mace
weapon->Light Mace.description: A light mace has a short wooden or metal shaft ending with a dense metal head. Used much like a club, it delivers heavy bludgeoning blows, but with extra power derived from the head's metal ridges or spikes.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Goblin Scuttle.name: Goblin Scuttle
action->Goblin Scuttle.description:
Trigger A goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to Brakknap

Effect Brakknap Steps.

action->Evoke Fault.name: Evoke Fault
action->Evoke Fault.description: The spirits of his ancestors tell Brakknap of a weakness of a foe within 30 feet. Until the end of his turn, Brakknap gains a +1 status bonus to the attack roll of his next Strike against that target, and that Strike deals an extra 1d6 precision damage. At the end of his turn, Brakknap becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 minute. If Brakknap uses Evoke Fault while clumsy, he increases the value of his clumsy condition by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3}.

action->Vision of Goblins' Fall.name: Vision of Goblins' Fall
action->Vision of Goblins' Fall.description:
Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect Brakknap touches a foe, making them experience the pain suffered by Chitterwood goblins during the Goblinblood Wars. The target takes @Damage[3d6[mental]] damage (@Check[will|dc:21|basic] save). A creature that fails the save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

Breathless Thicket.name: Breathless Thicket
Breathless Thicket.descriptionHazard: A 10-foot-wide patch of dead shrubs with wilted flowers steals the breath of the living.
Breathless Thicket.disable: @Check[nature|dc:28] (expert) or @Check[survival|dc:28] (expert) to recognize the thicket for what it is and avoid it
Breathless Thicket.reset: The thicket goes back into hibernation 1 round after there are no more targets to Strike in its reach. It then remains dormant for 24 hours.
Breathless Thicket.routine: (3 actions) The breathless thickets patch attempts to Strike a creature in its reach. It then uses Suffocate if it has any creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}; otherwise, it continues to Strike.
Breathless Thicket.stealth: (expert; or –10 and no minimum proficiency if the thicket has been recognized)

action->Branch Ambush.name: Branch Ambush
action->Branch Ambush.description:
Trigger A creature that breathes steps within 5 feet of a thicket

Effect The thicket makes a sinewy leaves Strike against the triggering creature. The hazard then rolls initiative.

action->Branch Grab.name: Branch Grab
action->Branch Grab.description: If the breathless thicket hits a creature with its leaves, that creature becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the thicket. Unlike with a normal Grab, the creature isn't @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized}, but it can't move beyond the reach of the thicket's leaves. A creature can sever a branch by Striking against AC 18 and dealing at least @Damage[12[slashing]] damage. A branch doesn't have the thicket's Hardness, and damaging it doesn't deal damage to the thicket ([[/act escape dc=26]]).

action->Suffocate.name: Suffocate
action->Suffocate.description:
Requirements The breathless thicket has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} one or more creatures that breathe

Effect The dead flowers of the breathless thicket begin pulling oxygen from its victims' lungs. Each creature grabbed by the breathless thicket takes @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]|traits:nonlethal]{4d6 nonlethal bludgeoning} damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage and can immediately attempt to [[/act escape dc=26]] as a free action.

Success The creature takes half damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is stupefied 1 for 1 round, and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. For as long as it's grabbed by the thicket, the creature must hold its breath or suffocate.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} and starts suffocating. If it's no longer grabbed, the creature stops suffocating.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).name: Sturdy Shield (Minor)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 64, and BT 32.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

equipment->Religious Symbol (Asmodeus).name: Religious Symbol (Asmodeus)
equipment->Religious Symbol (Asmodeus).description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->+2 Status to Will Saves vs. Ment.name: +2 Status to Will Saves vs. Mental

action->Godclaw Aura.name: Godclaw Aura
action->Godclaw Aura.description: 15 feet. Followers of any deities on the Godclaw pantheon or of the pantheon itself within the aura immediately know Caeto is a champion of the Godclaw.

action->Steel Command.name: Steel Command
action->Steel Command.description:
Trigger A creature in Caeto's aura damages him

Effect Caeto commands his enemy to kneel in supplication. The triggering creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:30] save. They then become immune to Steel Command for 1 minute.

Success The creature must choose to drop @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} as a free action or take @Damage[3d6[mental],1d8[persistent,spirit]] damage.

Failure The creature must choose to drop prone as a free action and be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} or take @Damage[6d6[mental],2d8[persistent,spirit]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature drops prone, is slowed 1, and takes @Damage[12d6[mental],2d8[persistent,spirit]] damage

action->Hand of the Avenger.name: Hand of the Avenger
action->Hand of the Avenger.description: Caeto evokes his oath to bring justice to those he deems a threat to Isger. Until the end of his turn, Caeto can use his lay on hands to damage an enemy in his aura as if they were undead, dealing spirit damage instead of vitality damage.

action->Lawbreaker's Bane.name: Lawbreaker's Bane
action->Lawbreaker's Bane.description: Caeto gains a +4 status bonus on damage to Strikes against targets that he knows have broken the law. The bonus increases to +6 if the target is a known member of the Hellbreakers League.

action->Oppressive Might.name: Oppressive Might
action->Oppressive Might.description: Caeto uses his posture to assert dominance over the masses. He attempts an Intimidation check to [[/act demoralize]] all non-allied creatures within 60 feet. He doesn't take a penalty against targets who don't understand his language. On a critical success, that target doesn't become temporarily immune to Caeto's other attempts to Demoralize them.

action->Subdue Fugitive.name: Subdue Fugitive
action->Subdue Fugitive.description:
Frequency once per minute

Requirements Caeto is wielding his longsword

Effect Caeto uses his shifting rune's Shift Weapon to transform his sword into a flail and makes a melee Strike with it. If the Strike hits and deals damage, he can attempt an Athletics check as a free action to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Trip} the target of the Strike. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward Caeto's multiple attack penalty.

Cannibalization of the Priestess.name: Cannibalization of the Priestess
Cannibalization of the Priestess.descriptionHazard: Spectral undead converge on any living creatures to rip into and devour them.
Cannibalization of the Priestess.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:28] to push past or run away, or @Check[occultism|dc:28] or @Check[religion|dc:28] (expert) to exorcise the haunt
Cannibalization of the Priestess.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after there are no bleeding creatures left in M2. If disabled, it resets after 1 day. Destroying Illcayna is the only way to permanently destroy this haunt.
Cannibalization of the Priestess.routine: (1 action) The haunt converges upon all creatures currently bleeding in area M2. The targeted creatures must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:28|basic] save against @Damage[3d6[spirit],3d6[void]] damage from the spirits pulling and gnawing on their souls. On a critical failure, the creature also suffers @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

action->Swarm.name: Swarm
action->Swarm.description:
Trigger A living creature enters the square of the town center

Effect All creatures within area M2 take @Damage[3d10[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:28|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

weapon->Heavy Crossbow.name: Heavy Crossbow
weapon->Heavy Crossbow.description: This large crossbow is harder to load and more substantial than a regular crossbow, but it packs a greater punch.

weapon->Rapier.name: Rapier
weapon->Rapier.description: The rapier is a long and thin piercing blade with a basket hilt. It is prized among many as a dueling weapon.

armor->Studded Leather Armor.name: Studded Leather Armor
armor->Studded Leather Armor.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Captain's Edge.name: Captain's Edge
action->Captain's Edge.description: Lamperia deals an additional 1d8 precision damage with Strikes when aboard her ship.

action->Hell's Glare.name: Hell's Glare
action->Hell's Glare.description: Lamperia's eyes glow like the fires of Hell as she swings her blade. Lamperia makes a rapier Strike. If she hits and deals damage, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical hit).

ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Gang Determination.description: When a Chelaxian loyalist is adjacent to at least one other ally, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves.

weapon->Heavy Crossbow.description: This large crossbow is harder to load and more substantial than a regular crossbow, but it packs a greater punch.

weapon->Morningstar.description: This weapon has a short shaft ending in a metal ball studded with spikes.

armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Gang Determination.name: Gang Determination
action->Gang Determination.description: When a Chelaxian loyalist is adjacent to at least one other ally, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves.

action->For Cheliax!.name: For Cheliax!
action->For Cheliax!.description: The Chelaxian loyalist leader Strides, shouting a Chelaxian slogan of loyalty. If they end the movement adjacent to a foe, they make a morningstar Strike against that foe. Each ally who hears and sees the loyalist leader attack can spend their reaction to Stride toward that foe.

weapon->Guisarme.name: Guisarme
weapon->Guisarme.description: This polearm bears a long, often one-sided, curved blade with a hook protruding from the blunt side of the blade, which can allow its wielder to trip opponents at a distance. Its shaft is usually 8 feet long.

weapon->Shortbow.name: Shortbow
weapon->Shortbow.description: This smaller bow is made of a single piece of wood and favored by skirmishers and cavalry.

armor->Chain Shirt.name: Chain Shirt
armor->Chain Shirt.description: Sometimes called a hauberk, this is a long shirt constructed of the same metal rings as chain mail. However, it is much lighter than chain mail and protects only the torso, upper arms, and upper legs of its wearer.

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

action->Punish the Fallen.name: Punish the Fallen
action->Punish the Fallen.description:
Requirements The Chelaxian recruit's last action was a successful @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Trip} against a foe

Effect The Chelaxian recruit makes a guisarme Strike against the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} foe. This attack is at the same multiple attack penalty as the Trip, but they increase their multiple attack penalty as usual after the Strike has been resolved.

Coarti Spy.name: Coarti Spy
Coarti Spy.publicNotes:
The burning-winged coartis are marked by the onerous contracts they agreed to and bound to Hell by the machinations of a legalistic devil, usually a phistophilus. Some coartis are truly a type of fallen celestial, those trapped in horrible agreements for the greater good or due to grievous errors in judgment, but most arise from mortal souls that were on the path to become celestials yet were contractually bound to Hell. Coartis serve in public roles as messengers and personal attendants to demonstrate the power of Hell and the legal acumen of their corruptors.

Celestials universally pity coartis and despise their creators, but their preferred methods of dealing with the corrupted beings differ. While angels argue against lost cases in Pharasma's courts, seeking loopholes in the voluminous contracts, azatas scheme daring heists against the contract storehouses. Archons are the most direct, working to end their blighted lives.

Masters of corruption and architects of conquest, devils seek both to tempt mortal life to join in their pursuit of all things profane and to spread tyranny throughout all worlds. The temptations they offer mortals range from great powers granted by the signing of an infernal contract to twisted favors following a whispered pledge to a diabolic patron, or any number of even subtler exchanges. Those who succumb to these temptations find themselves consigned to an afterlife of endless torment in the pits of Hell, wherein the only hope of escape lies in the chance of being promoted to become a devil in the infernal ranks.

Every devil has a specific role to play in the upkeep of the remorseless bureaucratic machine that is Hell, from soldiers and scholars to inquisitors, lawyers, judges, and executioners. Lowly orts perform subservient labor to more powerful and specialized devils, such as infantry and contract devils, while the greatest nessaris command entire infernal armies.

Asmodeus stands at the apex of the structure he created, but the layers below him are marked by a constant jockeying for position. Most diabolic plans ultimately serve to improve the schemer's place in the hierarchy.
Coarti Spy.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Blood Contract.name: Blood Contract
action->Blood Contract.description: When the coarti takes damage from their holy weakness, blood flows freely from their eyes and the contract carved into their skin. They take @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage and are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} as long as the persistent damage continues, but their Despairing Shriek recharges.

action->Despairing Shriek.name: Despairing Shriek
action->Despairing Shriek.description:
The coarti lets out a terrible cry, dealing @Damage[4d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[emanation|distance:30] with a @Check[will|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. Holy creatures that fail this save are also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}; this added effect has the emotion, fear, and mental traits.

The coarti can't use Despairing Shriek again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Despairing Shriek]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The coarti spy deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Wing Snap.name: Wing Snap
action->Wing Snap.description:
Frequency once per turn

Effect The coarti makes two wing Strikes, then falls if it's flying. It can't Fly until the end of its turn.

Creatures of the Night.name: Creatures of the Night
Creatures of the Night.descriptionHazard: Bats and rats swarm around Ezio's enemies.
Creatures of the Night.disable: @Check[nature|dc:24] or @Check[religion|dc:24] (trained) to scatter the vermin; two successes are required to disable it
Creatures of the Night.routine: (1 action) The bats and rats bite all creatures in the room, doing @Damage[(2d8+3)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). On a failure, the creature also takes @Damage[3[persistent,bleed]] damage.

action->Infest.name: Infest
action->Infest.description:
Trigger Ezio summons the swarms

Effect The swarms coalesce in nauseating fashion. Each creature in the room must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure). The hazard then rolls initiative.

weapon->Scythe.name: Scythe
weapon->Scythe.description: Derived from a farming tool used to mow down long grains and cereals, this weapon has a long wooden shaft with protruding handles, capped with a curved blade set at a right angle.

equipment->Religious Symbol of Urgathoa.name: Religious Symbol of Urgathoa
equipment->Religious Symbol of Urgathoa.description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity as a divine focus to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol must be held in one hand to use it.

action->Denounce Heretic.name: Denounce Heretic
action->Denounce Heretic.description:
Trigger A creature within 60 feet who the deathless acolyte can see and who doesn't worship Urgathoa casts a divine spell

Effect The deathless acolyte attempts to counteract the triggering spell using their spell attack modifier and a counteract rank of 2. If the deathless acolyte fails to counteract the spell, the caster is temporarily immune to further uses of Denounce Heretic for 24 hours.

action->Field of Undeath.name: Field of Undeath
action->Field of Undeath.description: 20 feet. Allies within the aura who have void healing gain fast healing 2. When a creature within the aura would receive vitality healing, it must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] or receive no healing from the effect.

Disarming Dummy.name: Disarming Dummy
Disarming Dummy.descriptionHazard: A leather strap on the dummy's mechanical arm swings around, knocking items out the hands of anyone adjacent.
Disarming Dummy.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to jam the rotor or @Check[athletics|dc:25] to knock the dummy over

action->Hold On!.name: Hold On!
action->Hold On!.description:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the trap

Effect The trap makes a single Athletics check with a +17 modifier to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} the creature.

Draconic Fumecrux.name: Draconic Fumecrux
Draconic Fumecrux.publicNotes:
This creature is the result of the fumecrux ritual performed on the head of a young adamantine dragon. Unlike most fumecruxes, draconic fumecruxes retain some of the unique magic of their living counterparts.

By studying the phenomena of hellcrowns and beheaded, Chelaxian mages have created a fusion of the two. Years of experimentation culminated in the creation of fumecruxes, brimstone specters capped with the decapitated and rotting heads of great beasts. The process is being attempted more frequently as yet another weapon for the war against Andoran.

action->Desiccated Boulder Breath.name: Desiccated Boulder Breath
action->Desiccated Boulder Breath.description:
The draconic fumecrux belches forth a mass of stones that are partially solid and partially void energy. These stones deal @Damage[4d8[bludgeoning],4d8[void]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save).

It can't use Desiccated Boulder Breath again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Desiccated Boulder Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Hellfume Body.name: Hellfume Body
action->Hellfume Body.description: A fumecrux's body consists of hellish fumes infused with void energy. When a creature that's holding its breath while it has been Swallowed Whole by a fumecrux attacks or casts a spell on a turn, the creature loses 3 rounds of air instead of 2.

action->Spit Up.name: Spit Up
action->Spit Up.description:
Requirements The draconic fumecrux has a creature Swallowed Whole

Effect The draconic fumecrux spits out the swallowed creature, flinging it through the air up to 40 feet away. The creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to the distance it traveled and lands @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If it lands in the same space as another creature, the other creature is pushed 5 feet and takes @Damage[(3d8+4)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). The fumecrux can't Spit Up more than one creature at a time no matter their size, and the most recently swallowed creature is Spit Up first.

Drude.name: Drude
Drude.publicNotes:
A drude is a gorga who embodies the fear of haunted places at night. This shadowy, shapeshifting fey is drawn to places of great tragedy, loss, or conflict, where they prey on their victim's fear of their surroundings. A drude takes perverse pleasure in toying with their quarry, whom they terrorize and exhaust over the course of the night. Malicious but not truly malevolent, a drude typically stops short of causing death since that would put an end to their fun. When faced with a situation they can't win, this fey is more likely to flee than fight.

A drude's true form resembles an emaciated, tailless fox made of darkness and ghostly shadows, though a drude rarely reveals this true self to others. When a drude has set their sights on a target, they prefer to shift into a form better suited to infiltrating the victim's sleeping space, such as a stray dog, a nearby shrub, or an item nestled among the target's gear.

If the drude can't find a suitable location for their fun, they'll make one. They find the most isolated area they can and put on a great show to bring out the fear of any residents. The unfortunate people in the drude's chosen territory are stalked by strange figures, assailed by wailing sounds, and haunted by terrible nightmares—all tricks by the shapeshifting drude to create the perfect environment for themself. Very rarely, a drude might become attached to a particular victim and follow them to other locations, choosing to haunt them instead of a singular location.

action->Lay Bare.name: Lay Bare
action->Lay Bare.description: When a drude using Change Shape is reduced to fewer than half their Hit Points, they revert to their true form.

action->Nightmare Herald.name: Nightmare Herald
action->Nightmare Herald.description: 90 feet. A creature that sleeps within the drude's aura is tormented with horrific nightmares and must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. On a failure, it's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 24 hours when it awakens. On a critical failure, it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued}.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
The drude takes on the appearance of a Small animal or inanimate object. This doesn't affect their statistics, but it could change the damage type of their Strikes while in animal form.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Haunting Echoes.name: Haunting Echoes
action->Haunting Echoes.description:
The drude targets a creature they can see within 30 feet and assaults it with illusions of ghosts and nightly terrors. The creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.

Success The creature takes @Damage[1d6[mental]] damage.

Failure The creature takes @Damage[1d6[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Critical Failure The creature takes @Damage[2d6[mental]] damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

weapon->Shortbow.name: Shortbow
weapon->Shortbow.description: This smaller bow is made of a single piece of wood and favored by skirmishers and cavalry.

weapon->Shortsword.name: Shortsword
weapon->Shortsword.description: These blades come in a variety of shapes and styles, but they are typically 2 feet long.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

action->Drunken Stumble.name: Drunken Stumble
action->Drunken Stumble.description:
Trigger The drunken Isgeri Slayer is hit with an attack that deals physical damage

Effect The drunken slayer stumbles away from the blow. They gain resistance 2 to the damage of the triggering attack and they can Step. They also take a –1 circumstance penalty to AC for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.QVLX06QevxTcuZxU]{Effect: Drunken Stumble}

action->Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
action->Hunt Prey.description:
The Isgeri Slayer designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're Tracking, as their prey.

The slayer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [[/act seek]] the prey and to Survival checks to [[/act track]] the prey.

The first time the slayer hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d6 precision damage. The slayer also ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment. These effects last until the poacher uses Hunt Prey again.

Elite Ort.name: Elite Ort
Elite Ort.publicNotes:
These shapeless masses of quivering flesh are the least of devilkind. Pathetic creatures from Hell's first layer, orts roam alongside damned and suffering souls. Nessaris can combine the essence of a mob of orts to concentrate their collective wickedness within a single form, granting it an infernal sentience and transforming it into a more powerful devil (see the nessari's Shape Devils ability).

Masters of corruption and architects of conquest, devils seek both to tempt mortal life to join in their pursuit of all things profane and to spread tyranny throughout all worlds. The temptations they offer mortals range from great powers granted by the signing of an infernal contract to twisted favors following a whispered pledge to a diabolic patron, or any number of even subtler exchanges. Those who succumb to these temptations find themselves consigned to an afterlife of endless torment in the pits of Hell, wherein the only hope of escape lies in the chance of being promoted to become a devil in the infernal ranks.

Every devil has a specific role to play in the upkeep of the remorseless bureaucratic machine that is Hell, from soldiers and scholars to inquisitors, lawyers, judges, and executioners. Lowly orts perform subservient labor to more powerful and specialized devils, such as infantry and contract devils, while the greatest nessaris command entire infernal armies.

Asmodeus stands at the apex of the structure he created, but the layers below him are marked by a constant jockeying for position. Most diabolic plans ultimately serve to improve the schemer's place in the hierarchy.

action->Fiendish Order.name: Fiendish Order
action->Fiendish Order.description: 30 feet. An elite ort is ripe enough to be shaped into another, more powerful devil and gives off an aura of superiority. Each allied ort in the aura gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls.

action->Subservience.name: Subservience
action->Subservience.description:
Orts have little drive of their own, but other devils can take command of them. A non-ort devil can issue a command to all orts within 60 feet of it with a single action, which has the auditory and concentrate traits. The devil picks one of the following orders orts can understand, and the orts follow that order. The command and its effects end once the commander is out of the ort's sight, when a new command is issued by the same or another devil, or when the ort dies.

  • Kill The ort attacks one target the commander singles out and gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against the target.
  • Defend The ort circles the commander and attacks any creature that comes near. It gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saves.
  • Fetch The ort gains a +10–foot circumstance bonus to its Speed and attempts to get an object or person the commander singles out. It attacks anyone and anything that gets in the way.
  • Work The ort performs drudge work dictated by the commander.

Erinys Sister Wight.name: Erinys Sister Wight
Erinys Sister Wight.publicNotes:
Wights are intelligent undead spawned through inescapable cycles of spite. This spite might come from their own malevolent will in life, or can be instilled by necromantic rituals, typically involving the desecration of burial sites. Wights usually haunt burial grounds, catacombs, or other places of the dead. Their hunger is targeted toward the living—those whose pumping hearts and ruddy warmth inspire visceral hatred.

As many types of wights exist as types of people from which they might be created. Hulking brutes, skittering sneaks, and cunning tinkers all make for different wights with different niches to fill. The environment, too, plays a part in determining a wight's special abilities and defenses. Frost wights, for instance, can be found in parts of the world where exposure is a common end and the resentment of being left alone in the wild is a common source of spite. Durable and sustained by void energy, wights can last in harsh environments without decaying the way some lesser undead do.

A single wight can wreak significant havoc if it is compelled to rise from its tomb. Because creatures slain under a wight's curse can become wights as well, all it takes is a single wight and a handful of unlucky graveyard visitors to create a veritable horde of these undead. Thus, canny priests and adventurers know that the best solution to a wight problem is swift and total eradication. Care must be taken, though, to destroy wight spawn before attempting to destroy the parent wight, for spawn without a master gain the ability to create spawn of their own.

weapon->Dart.name: Dart
weapon->Dart.description: This thrown weapon is larger than an arrow but shorter than a javelin. It typically has a short shaft of wood ending in a metal tip and is sometimes stabilized by feathers or fur.

action->Grave Weapon.name: Grave Weapon
action->Grave Weapon.description: The wight is bound to the darts she was buried with.

action->Final Spite.name: Final Spite
action->Final Spite.description:
Trigger The wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. This Strike can inflict corrupting spite, but fueled by spite doesn't apply.

action->Fueled by Spite.name: Fueled by Spite
action->Fueled by Spite.description: Each time a creature loses Hit Points due to a corrupting spite curse the wight inflicted, the wight gains 3 temporary Hit Points.

action->Claw of Pain.name: Claw of Pain
action->Claw of Pain.description: When the sister wight targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can cause lingering pain. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,mental]] damage (and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a critical failure).

action->Corrupting Spite.name: Corrupting Spite
action->Corrupting Spite.description:
The wight's unarmed attacks and bound weapons inflict a curse that makes a creature grow weak and spiteful. If a wight inflicts corrupting spite on a creature already afflicted by it, the victim attempts a new save, ignoring the result if it's better than a failure.

A living humanoid that dies while under the curse rises as a wight after [[/gmr 1d4 #Wight Spawn Timer]]{1d4 rounds}, controlled by the wight that killed it. The wight spawn can't inflict corrupting spite and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}. If its creator dies or after roughly a month of existence, the new wight becomes autonomous and turns into a normal wight

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:17];

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and doesn't treat any creatures as allies (1 round)

Stage 3 As stage 2, except @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3} (1 round)

Stage 4 As stage 2, except @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4} (1 round).

action->Flurry of Blows.name: Flurry of Blows
action->Flurry of Blows.description: The sister wight makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

Ezio Gaeta.name: Ezio Gaeta
Ezio Gaeta.hpDetails: coffin restoration, fast healing 7, void healing

equipment->Devil Costume.name: Devil Costume
equipment->Devil Costume.description: Fine clothing, suitable for a noble or royal, is made with expensive fabrics, precious metals, and intricate patterns.

action->Children of the Night.name: Children of the Night
action->Children of the Night.description: Ezio's presence brings forth creatures of the night to do his bidding. Ezio can give telepathic orders to these creatures as long as they're within 100 feet, but they can't communicate back. Ezio prefers to keep the company of rat swarms.

action->Cloud of Plague.name: Cloud of Plague
action->Cloud of Plague.description:
Trigger Ezio is reduced to 0 HP

Effect Ezio's body explodes in a bloody mist that carries a deadly plague. Each living creature in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save or contract choking death at stage 1 (stage 2 on a critical failure). Ezio bursts forth from the corpse of the first creature to die of this affliction, fully healed, but if all affected creatures are cured of the disease, Ezio is permanently destroyed.

action->Coffin Restoration.name: Coffin Restoration
action->Coffin Restoration.description: Unlike other undead, a vampire isn't destroyed at 0 HP. Instead, it falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If its body rests in its coffin for 1 hour, the vampire gains 1 HP, after which its fast healing begins to function normally.

action->Hypnotic Gaze.name: Hypnotic Gaze
action->Hypnotic Gaze.description: 30 feet. Even when he's not attempting to Dominate a creature, Ezio uses his power over others' minds to subtly befuddle them, helping enable his plots. Creatures in the aura take a –1 status penalty to Perception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1xRFPTFtWtGJ9ELw]{Sense Motive} and Perception DCs against Ezio. When a creature ends its turn in Ezio's aura, it must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. On a failure, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round on a critical failure).

action->Vampire Vulnerabilities.name: Vampire Vulnerabilities
action->Vampire Vulnerabilities.description:
All vampires possess the following vulnerabilities.

  • Compulsions Vampires are creatures with strange and unknowable compulsions. A typical vampire can't voluntarily cross running water unless they're transported while they hide within their coffin, nor can they enter a private dwelling unless invited in by someone with the authority to do so. At your discretion, vampires might have different compulsions—a pirate vampire might not be able to set foot on solid ground without being invited, for example. The vampire can still be forced to do these things and might be able to overcome their compulsion just as they do their revulsion.
  • Revulsion A vampire can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of brandished garlic or a brandished religious symbol of a deity with a holy sanctification option. To brandish garlic or a religious symbol, a creature must Interact to do so, and it remains brandished for 1 round (similar to Raising a Shield). If the vampire involuntarily comes within 10 feet of an object of their revulsion, they gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, running from the object of their revulsion until they end an action beyond 10 feet. After 1 round of being exposed to the subject of their revulsion, a vampire can attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save as a single action, which has the concentrate trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • Stake A wooden stake driven through a vampire's heart drops the vampire to 0 HP and prevents them from healing above 0 HP, even in their coffin. Staking a vampire requires 3 actions and works only if the vampire is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If the stake is removed, the vampire can heal above 0 HP again, and if they're in their coffin, the 1-hour rest period begins once the stake is removed. If the vampire's head is severed and anointed with holy water while the stake is in place, the vampire is destroyed.
  • Sunlight If exposed to direct sunlight, a vampire immediately becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}. The slowed value increases by 1 each time the vampire ends their turn in sunlight, and the condition ends when they're no longer in sunlight. If the vampire loses all their actions in this way, they're destroyed. Due to their supernatural aversion to light, vampires don't cast shadows or show a reflection in mirrors

action->Biting Flies.name: Biting Flies
action->Biting Flies.description: Each creature that shares a space with Ezio takes @Damage[3d8[piercing]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save. On a critical failure, Ezio also gains the benefits of Drinking that creature's Blood.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
Ezio transforms into a swarm of Tiny flies. While in this form, he has the swarm trait and gains weakness 7 to area and splash damage. He has a land Speed of 20 feet and a fly Speed of 30 feet, and can fit into spaces only a few inches wide, moving his constituent creatures through the gap. He can't make any of his normal Strikes but gains the Biting Flies action.

Biting Flies 1 Each creature that shares a space with Ezio takes @Damage[3d8[piercing]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save. On a critical failure, Ezio also gains the benefits of Drinking that creature's Blood.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Create Servitor.name: Create Servitor
action->Create Servitor.description:
If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by Drink Blood, Ezio can turn this victim into a vampire servitor by donating some of their own blood to the victim and burying the victim in earth for 3 nights.

A vampiric servitor is compelled to obey its creator, but if Ezio controls too many vampiric servitors at once (as determined by the GM), or if the servitor is a higher level than Ezio that created it, strong-willed servitors can free themselves by succeeding at a @Check[will|dc:25] saving throw.

action->Dominate.name: Dominate
action->Dominate.description:
Ezio can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.OsOhx3TGIZ7AhD0P]{Dominate} at will as a divine innate spell. Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the visual trait (@Check[will|dc:23]). A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours.

Fully destroying Ezio ends the domination, but merely reducing Ezio to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell.

action->Drink Blood.name: Drink Blood
action->Drink Blood.description:
Requirement A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or willing creature is within Ezio's reach.

Effect Ezio sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an @Check[athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions.

The victim is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and Ezio regains HP equal to 10% of its maximum HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to Ezio but increases the victim's drained value by 1, killing the victim when it reaches drained 5.

A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a @Check[medicine|dc:20|name:Blood Transfusion] check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drain by 1 after 10 minutes.

action->Turn to Mist.name: Turn to Mist
action->Turn to Mist.description:
Ezio turns into a cloud of vapor, as the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.V8wXOsoejQhe6CyG]{Vapor Form} spell, or back to its normal form. Ezio loses fast healing while in gaseous form.

Ezio can remain in this form indefinitely.

Falling Crates.name: Falling Crates
Falling Crates.descriptionHazard: A tower of precariously stacked crates is rigged to fall when someone steps through a trip wire.
Falling Crates.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to carefully remove the trip wire.

action->Tumble Down.name: Tumble Down
action->Tumble Down.description:
Trigger A creature trips the wire

Effect Several heavy crates come crashing down in the 10-foot square area marked on the map, dealing @Damage[(2d10+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in that area (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save). A creature who critically fails the save is knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

False Hellbreaker.name: False Hellbreaker
False Hellbreaker.publicNotes:
Guards are rank-and-file members of a town watch or city guard, trained to look for trouble, take down criminals, and follow orders.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Sap.name: Sap
weapon->Sap.description: A sap has a soft wrapping around a dense core, typically a leather sheath around a lead rod. Its head is wider than its grip to disperse the force of a blow, as the weapon's purpose is to knock out its victim rather than to draw blood.

armor->Scale Mail.name: Scale Mail
armor->Scale Mail.description: Scale mail consists of many metal scales sewn onto a reinforced leather backing, often in the form of a long shirt that protects the torso, arms, and legs.

equipment->Signal Whistle.name: Signal Whistle
equipment->Signal Whistle.description: When sounded, a signal whistle can be heard clearly up to half a mile away across open terrain.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->+1 Bonus on Perception to Find c.name: +1 Bonus on Perception to Find concealed objects

action->Block the Path.name: Block the Path
action->Block the Path.description: The false Hellbreaker prepares to harry anyone who tries to move past them. If it hits a creature with a Reactive Strike triggered by a move action, the triggering creature treats any squares within the steward's guard's reach as difficult terrain until the start of the false Hellbreaker's next turn.

Fiendish Viro Ahala.name: Fiendish Viro Ahala
Fiendish Viro Ahala.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic; +16 Ref vs. area effects

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->+3 Status to Reflex Saves vs. Ar.name: +3 Status to Reflex Saves vs. Area Effects

action->Retributive Glare.name: Retributive Glare
action->Retributive Glare.description:
Trigger A creature within 60 feet damages Viro

Effect Viro burns the one that hurt them with his gaze. He makes a flaming gaze Strike against the triggering creature. This ranged attack doesn't trigger reactions, and if it's a critical hit, the target also takes @Damage[1d8[persistent,fire]] damage.

action->Furious Viper Cut.name: Furious Viper Cut
action->Furious Viper Cut.description:
Requirements Viro's last action was a longsword Strike that dealt damage

Effect The sword transforms into a snake and bites at the target of the required Strike. The target takes @Damage[2d6[poison]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:24|basic] save). On a failure, the target is also racked with terrible pain, becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2} on a critical failure).

action->Wrathful Strike.name: Wrathful Strike
action->Wrathful Strike.description: Viro makes a longsword Strike. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional die of weapon damage and 1d4 persistent bleed damage.

Food Fight.name: Food Fight
Food Fight.descriptionHazard: Ghostly food appears on cracked plates as they float above the table. The faint outline of children can occasionally be seen as the ectoplasmic food is flung throughout the room.
Food Fight.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:17] (trained) to weaken the spiritual energy in the room, @Check[acrobatics|dc:18] (trained) to pluck a floating plate out of the air, or @Check[intimidation|dc:19] (trained) to reprimand one of the ghostly children for their poor behavior; each successful check disables one of four plates
Food Fight.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the room or after all four plates are destroyed. After 1 hour, if at least one plate remains, the haunt reactivates. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day and the plates magically reassemble. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer in area D5 is destroyed.
Food Fight.routine: (4 actions) For each plate disabled or destroyed, the haunt has 1 fewer action. On each action, spectral food is flung off a plate at a different living creature in the room. This ranged Strike doesn't provoke reactions. A creature critically hit by a flung food Strike is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

action->Dinner Bell.name: Dinner Bell
action->Dinner Bell.description:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the set table

Effect A loud phantom dinner bell rings before the plates begin to tremble and hover. Each creature in the room takes @Damage[(2d6+2)[sonic]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:17|basic] save). The haunt then rolls initiative.

strike-melee->Flung Food.name: Flung Food
strike-melee->Flung Food.description: no range increment or multiple attack penalty

Gala Crowd.name: Gala Crowd
Gala Crowd.descriptionHazard: The panicked crowd of gala attendees reacts to the unexpected attack.
Gala Crowd.disable: @Check[intimidation|dc:19], @Check[diplomacy|dc:21], or @Check[athletics|dc:24] to get guests to move out of harm's way; four total successes across all skills are required to disable the hazard
Gala Crowd.routine:
(1 action) The crowd reacts in one of the following ways, determined by a d12 die roll:

  • 1–2 Hunker Down: The crowd freezes in fear. It provides lesser cover to all creatures in the great room for 1 round.
  • 3–4 Run!: Determine a random direction. Each creature in the area must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save or be moved 10 feet in that direction. On a critical failure, they're also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. This is forced movement.
  • 5–6 Hysteria: The crowd's dread is contagious. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure). This is an emotion, fear, and mental effect.
  • 7–8 Wailing: The cries make it hard to concentrate. For 1 round, any creature in the area that attempts an action with the concentrate trait must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:4] or the action is disrupted. This is an auditory effect.
  • 9–10 Crushing Press: Members of the crowd shove and push, trying to find a way to safety. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[(2d8+5)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:21|basic] save).

  • 11–12 Wild Dismay: The chaos is overwhelming. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} for 1 round. This is an emotion and mental effect.

action->Panic at the Gala.name: Panic at the Gala
action->Panic at the Gala.description:
Trigger A creature rolls initiative

Effect The crowd stampedes, dealing @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone on the great room floor (@Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save), then rolls initiative.

Gliding River Snake.name: Gliding River Snake
Gliding River Snake.publicNotes:
Wise travelers keep watch for snakes along their path, but few expect them to attack from above. When threatened, the gliding river snake takes to high ground and launches toward its target, gliding through the air by flattening its ribs and controlling its flight by moving sinuously. If unable to make an aerial attack, the gliding river snake simply attempts to bite its target and snap off a finger or two. The snake's jaws are extremely powerful, able to bite clean through bone and flesh.

The mildly venomous gliding river snake prefers to keep to itself but reacts harshly to potential threats with a vicious bite. Its mouth is filled with serrated teeth that tear into its prey rather than piercing like a typical snake. Using these teeth, the gliding river snake rends its food into chunks before swallowing, allowing it to target larger prey. Sometimes an unfortunate animal is beset by multiple gliding river snakes, covering it with writhing serpents. These group meals have been mistaken for a singular monster before, as only the snakes' tentacle-like bodies can be seen as they feast.

The gliding river snake often lives in pairs, staying together with its mate through the mating season before separating in the off season. They lay their eggs either in trees or underwater, sometimes even stealing bird nests for their own uses once they've devoured the birds' eggs. Many a birdwatcher has been taken aback by the sight of hatching snakes up in the treetops, occasionally even laid side by side with bird eggs for easy access to a meal.

action->Deep Breath.name: Deep Breath
action->Deep Breath.description: The gliding river snake can hold its breath for about an hour.

action->Aerial Ambush.name: Aerial Ambush
action->Aerial Ambush.description:
Requirements The gliding river snake is higher than a creature

Effect The gliding river snake launches up to 20 feet down toward a creature and makes a jaws Strike against it. If the snake hits, it doesn't take damage from the fall. If it misses, it takes only half the regular damage from the distance it fell.

action->Aquatic Ambush.name: Aquatic Ambush
action->Aquatic Ambush.description:
Requirements The gliding river snake is in water and a creature that hasn't detected it is within 20 feet

Effect The gliding river snake moves up to its swim Speed + 10 feet toward the triggering creature, traveling on water and on land. Once the creature is in reach, the snake makes a jaws Strike against it. The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against this Strike.

action->Weaving Glide.name: Weaving Glide
action->Weaving Glide.description: The snake glides toward the ground, 5 feet down and up to 20 feet forward through the air. As long as it spends at least 1 action gliding each round and hasn't yet reached the ground, it remains in the air at the end of its turn.

Godclaw Hellcrown.name: Godclaw Hellcrown
Godclaw Hellcrown.publicNotes:
Hellcrowns are a known hazard when fighting any order of Hellknights, especially so when those knights hail from the Order of the Godclaw. These hellcrowns seem to have a much better grasp on the necromancy that weaves through their existence and have been reported to form spectral weapons from their ghostly bodies that they can whip at their foes at a moment's notice. These are some of the most dangerous hellcrowns one can meet.

Those well-versed in the art of fighting against Hellknights warn their comrades to avoid decapitating their foes. This is because a Hellknight's unearthly loyalty can sometimes imbue their helmet with void energy, resulting in a restless spirit called a hellcrown. Hellcrowns float through the air, their ghostly bodies resembling smoky apparitions of the armor they once wore in life, and though they retain their twisted allegiance to their cause, little else remains of their former humanity and sense of self.

Hellcrowns don't join their living counterparts in armies or regiments, preferring to haunt the bloody battle sites where they originally fell. The strength of a hellcrown varies depending on the might of the living soldier who fell and the power of their enmity toward the cause of their death. In some cases, particularly powerful hellcrowns have access to spells and even form ghostly swarms to seek vengeance against any they deem responsible for their deaths.

action->Agony Lash.name: Agony Lash
action->Agony Lash.description: A creature damaged by the godclaw hellcrown's spectral lash Strike must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save. On a failure, the tip of the lash is embedded in the creature, dealing @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage and imparting a –5-foot status penalty to the target's Speeds for 1 minute. Each additional embedded tip increases the bleed damage by 1 and the penalty to Speed by 5 feet. A creature can remove a tip with an Interact action to reduce the amount of bleed damage and the penalty to Speed. Pulling out the last tip removes both conditions.

action->Forbidding Stare.name: Forbidding Stare
action->Forbidding Stare.description: All creatures that can see the hellcrown and are affected by agony lash must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] saving throw. A creature that fails is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}, and on a critical failure, it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for as long as it's frightened. Regardless of the result, the creature is then temporarily immune to Forbidding Stare for 10 minutes.

Gredress Tilnanos.name: Gredress Tilnanos
Gredress Tilnanos.publicNotes:
Demonologists can pull a creature from the Outer Rifts and bend it to their will... for a time.

Hidden secrets and occult powers have an irresistible lure for many. Since the majority of these NPCs are spellcasters, consider using alternative spell lists to adjust their themes.

weapon->Longspear.name: Longspear
weapon->Longspear.description: This very long spear, sometimes called a pike, is purely for thrusting rather than throwing. Used by many soldiers and city watch for crowd control and defense against charging enemies, it must be wielded with two hands.

armor->Robes.name: Robes
armor->Robes.description: Explorer's clothing is sturdy enough that it can be reinforced to protect you, even though it isn't a suit of armor. It comes in many forms, though the most common sorts look like clerical vestments, monk's garments, or wizard's robes, as members of all three classes are likely to avoid wearing armor.

armor->Hellknight Half Plate.name: Hellknight Half Plate
armor->Hellknight Half Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifers.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

equipment->Fiendish Hypotheses and Prote.name: Fiendish Hypotheses and Protections from Same (Spellbook)
equipment->Fiendish Hypotheses and Prote.description: A spellbook holds the written knowledge necessary to learn and prepare various spells, a necessity for wizards (who typically get one for free) and a useful luxury for other spellcasters looking to learn additional spells. Each spellbook can hold up to 100 spells. The Price listed is for a blank spellbook.

equipment->Signifer's Mask.name: Signifer's Mask
equipment->Signifer's Mask.description: This mask is often devoid of eyeholes or other decorative features. The mask doesn't obscure your vision, though it makes it impossible for others to see your eyes. While wearing your signifer's mask, you gain +1 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ewwCglB7XOPLUz72]{Lie}, Intimidation checks, and Deception DCs against @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.1xRFPTFtWtGJ9ELw]{Sense Motive}.

action->Diabolic Certitude.name: Diabolic Certitude
action->Diabolic Certitude.description:
Some signifers are so assured of their power to control devils that they sometimes reach too far. When Gredress publicly espouses the superiority of Hell, she gains a +1 status bonus to skill checks, AC, and saves for 1 day. These bonuses don't apply against devils. At the end of the day, Gredress must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save or take a –1 status penalty to skill checks, AC, and saves against devils for 1 day (or –2 on a critical failure).

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.6bGS19fnwbv4E7mn]{Effect: Diabolic Certitude}

action->Breach the Pit.name: Breach the Pit
action->Breach the Pit.description:
Requirements Gredress's last action was to cast a non-cantrip spell

Effect Gredress siphons energy drawn from Hell into her weapon. Until the end of the turn, the weapon deals an extra 2d6 damage.

Roll 1d20 to determine the type:

1–4 cold

5–12 fire

13–16 poison

17–20 void.

action->Devil Summoning.name: Devil Summoning
action->Devil Summoning.description: Gredress can cast a 5th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.29ytKctjg7qSW2ff]{Summon Fiend} arcane spell to summon a devil. To do so, she must sacrifice two 4th-rank prepared spells and voluntarily take @Damage[4d12[mental]] damage that can't be reduced or prevented. If Gredress is unable to Sustain the Spell, including if she's knocked out or killed, the spell continues, but the GM rolls a @Check[flat|dc:10] each round, ending the spell on a failure.

Hecatinia.name: Hecatinia
Hecatinia.publicNotes:
Often leading a small team of lower-ranking guards, watch officers patrol their assigned areas to maintain order and enforce laws. Watch officers get the job done, though their methods aren't always gentle or kind.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Warhammer.name: Warhammer
weapon->Warhammer.description: This weapon has a wooden shaft ending in a large, heavy metal head. The head of the hammer might be single-sided or double-sided, but it's always capable of delivering powerful bludgeoning blows.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
