armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

equipment->Signal Whistle.name: Signal Whistle
equipment->Signal Whistle.description: When sounded, a signal whistle can be heard clearly up to half a mile away across open terrain.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Air of Authority.name: Air of Authority
action->Air of Authority.description: 10 feet. Creatures in the aura who are the same or lower level than the watch officer take a -2 status penalty to their Will DC against the watch officer's attempts to [[/act coerce]] or [[/act demoralize]] them.

action->Bravery.name: Bravery
action->Bravery.description: When the watch officer rolls a success on a Will save against a fear effect, they get a critical success instead. In addition, any time they gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} condition, reduce its value by 1.

action->Hunter's Fatigue.name: Hunter's Fatigue
action->Hunter's Fatigue.description: Having fought undead nonstop for the entire day, both Hellknights have the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} condition. The condition goes away as usual with a full night's rest.

action->Sudden Charge.name: Sudden Charge
action->Sudden Charge.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The watch officer Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy.

action->Vampire Vulnerabilities.name: Vampire Vulnerabilities
action->Vampire Vulnerabilities.description:
All vampires possess the following vulnerabilities.

  • Compulsions Vampires are creatures with strange and unknowable compulsions. A typical vampire can't voluntarily cross running water unless they're transported while they hide within their coffin, nor can they enter a private dwelling unless invited in by someone with the authority to do so. At your discretion, vampires might have different compulsions—a pirate vampire might not be able to set foot on solid ground without being invited, for example. The vampire can still be forced to do these things and might be able to overcome their compulsion just as they do their revulsion.
  • Revulsion A vampire can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of brandished garlic or a brandished religious symbol of a deity with a holy sanctification option. To brandish garlic or a religious symbol, a creature must Interact to do so, and it remains brandished for 1 round (similar to Raising a Shield). If the vampire involuntarily comes within 10 feet of an object of their revulsion, they gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition, running from the object of their revulsion until they end an action beyond 10 feet. After 1 round of being exposed to the subject of their revulsion, a vampire can attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save as a single action, which has the concentrate trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for [[/gmr 1d6 #rounds]]{1d6 rounds} (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • Stake A wooden stake driven through a vampire's heart drops the vampire to 0 HP and prevents them from healing above 0 HP, even in their coffin. Staking a vampire requires 3 actions and works only if the vampire is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If the stake is removed, the vampire can heal above 0 HP again, and if they're in their coffin, the 1-hour rest period begins once the stake is removed. If the vampire's head is severed and anointed with holy water while the stake is in place, the vampire is destroyed.
  • Sunlight If exposed to direct sunlight, a vampire immediately becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}. The slowed value increases by 1 each time the vampire ends their turn in sunlight, and the condition ends when they're no longer in sunlight. If the vampire loses all their actions in this way, they're destroyed. Due to their supernatural aversion to light, vampires don't cast shadows or show a reflection in mirrors

action->Dominate.name: Dominate
action->Dominate.description:
The vampire can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.OsOhx3TGIZ7AhD0P]{Dominate} at will as a divine innate spell. Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the visual trait (@Check[will|dc:22]). A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that vampire's Dominate for 24 hours.

Fully destroying the vampire ends the domination, but merely reducing the vampire to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell.

action->Drink Blood.name: Drink Blood
action->Drink Blood.description:
Requirement A @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.6uEgoh53GbXuHpTF]{Paralyzed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}, or willing creature is within the vampire's reach.

Effect The vampire sinks its fangs into that creature to drink its blood. This requires an @Check[athletics|defense:fortitude] check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions.

The victim is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and the vampire regains @Damage[10[healing]] HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the vampire but increases the victim's drained value by 1, killing the victim when it reaches drained 5. A vampire can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but it gains no HP from doing so.

A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a @Check[medicine|dc:20|name:Blood Transfusion] check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drain by 1 after 10 minutes.

action->Spellstrike.name: Spellstrike
action->Spellstrike.description:
Frequency until recharged

Effect Hedvend VI channels a spell into his claw Strike to deliver a combined attack. He Casts a Spell that requires a spell attack roll. The effects of the spell don't occur immediately but are imbued into his attack instead. He attempts a claw melee Strike, with the attack roll determining the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty, but he doesn't apply the penalty until after he's completed the Spellstrike. The infusion of spell energy grants his Strike the arcane trait, making it magical. After he uses Spellstrike, he can't do so again until he recharges his Spellstrike as a single action, which has the concentrate trait. He also recharge his Spellstrike when he Drinks Blood.

Hellknight Field-Maralictor.name: Hellknight Field-Maralictor
Hellknight Field-Maralictor.publicNotes:
Elite fighters from the lowest ranks of nobility, knights are proud champions of their court. Unlike other nobles, knights must earn their title through loyalty and strength-of-arms rather than inheritance. Ideals such as chivalry, honor, and virtue are associated with knights but not all meet such romantic standards.

The denizens of a noble court are the most powerful people in a civilization, primed with wealth, station, and authority above the common people.

weapon->Bastard Sword.name: Bastard Sword
weapon->Bastard Sword.description: This broad-bladed sword, sometimes called the hand-and-a-half sword, has a longer grip so it can be held in one hand or used with two hands to provide extra slashing power.

weapon->Spear.name: Spear
weapon->Spear.description: A long metal shaft ending with a metal spike, a spear can be used one-handed as a melee weapon and can be thrown.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Full Plate (with Livery).name: Full Plate (with Livery)
armor->Full Plate (with Livery).description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

action->Hellknight's Rigor.name: Hellknight's Rigor
action->Hellknight's Rigor.description: Any time the Hellknight field-maralictor gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} condition, they reduce its value by 1.

action->Quick Shield Block.name: Quick Shield Block
action->Quick Shield Block.description:
The Hellknight field-maralictor has an additional reaction they can use only to Shield Block.

They can Shield Block for an adjacent ally, preventing that ally from taking damage instead of protecting themself.

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ShieldBlock]

action->Intimidating Strike.name: Intimidating Strike
action->Intimidating Strike.description: The Hellknight field-maralictor makes a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical hit.

action->Maralictor's Command.name: Maralictor's Command
action->Maralictor's Command.description: The Hellknight field-maralictor shouts a command to an ally of their choice. That ally can spend a reaction to Stride and Strike. The ally becomes immune to Maralictor's Command for 24 hours.

action->Warding Shift.name: Warding Shift
action->Warding Shift.description:
Requirements The Hellknight fieldmaralictor is adjacent to a willing ally

Effect The Hellknight field-maralictor moves an adjacent willing ally 5 feet in any direction and can Step into the space the ally vacated.

Hestlebeth.name: Hestlebeth
Hestlebeth.publicNotes:
A jailer's primary responsibility is to keep prisoners from escaping. Jailers must often use force, or the threat of force, to keep their charges in line, as even the most carefully crafted cells, manacles, or chains can fail with time and persistence when the prisoners have the will to attempt an escape.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.

weapon->Club.name: Club
weapon->Club.description: This is a piece of stout wood shaped or repurposed to bludgeon an enemy. Clubs can be intricately carved pieces of martial art or as simple as a tree branch or piece of wood.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

armor->Studded Leather Armor.name: Studded Leather Armor
armor->Studded Leather Armor.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

equipment->Manacles (Simple).name: Manacles (Simple)
equipment->Manacles (Simple).description: You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an exploration activity taking 10-30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] any time it uses a manipulate action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is immobilized. For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from simple manacles requires three successful @Check[thievery|dc:22|traits:action:pick-a-lock] checks.

equipment->Signal Whistle.name: Signal Whistle
equipment->Signal Whistle.description: When sounded, a signal whistle can be heard clearly up to half a mile away across open terrain.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->+1 Bonus on Perception to Find C.name: +1 Bonus on Perception to Find Concealed Objects

action->Efficient Capture.name: Efficient Capture
action->Efficient Capture.description:
Requirements The jailer has manacles in hand and is adjacent to a creature

Effect The jailer attempts to bind the creature's wrists or ankles with the manacles. If the jailer succeeds at an attack roll with a [[/r 1d20+9 #Manacles]]{+9 modifier} against the target's AC, they apply the manacles.

action->Hunter's Fatigue.name: Hunter's Fatigue
action->Hunter's Fatigue.description: Having fought undead nonstop for the entire day, both Hellknights have the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.HL2l2VRSaQHu9lUw]{Fatigued} condition. The condition goes away as usual with a full night's rest.

action->Intimidating Strike.name: Intimidating Strike
action->Intimidating Strike.description: The jailer makes a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical hit.

action->Subdue Prisoners.name: Subdue Prisoners
action->Subdue Prisoners.description: The jailer doesn't take the normal penalty for making a nonlethal attack when attacking with their club.

Illcayna Alonnor.name: Illcayna Alonnor
Illcayna Alonnor.allSavesBonus: +1 status bonus to all saves vs. holy effects

weapon->Reaper's Sigh.name: Reaper's Sigh
weapon->Reaper's Sigh.description:
Aspects: death

This +1 wounding striking scythe is a weapon of intricately embellished blackened metals and polished surfaces. It was originally intended to venerate Urgathoa in her role as the herald of autumn in pre-Taldor Isger but has recently found more malevolent meaning. With the scythe in her hands, Urgathoa was said to cull the harvest for feast to usher in the cold, bleak winters. As Urgathoa's legends took a darker tone, the scythe's own legend was reclaimed to venerate Erastil and Pharasma as part of the holiday for which it now claims a name.

Minor Gifts: death gaze, living death

Major Gifts: pierce the veil, tide of death

Grand Gift: grim specter

equipment->Bands of Force.name: Bands of Force
equipment->Bands of Force.description:
Decorated with clear gemstones, these thick metal bands spread an inflexible layer of force over your body. The force grants you a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5 as armor. You can affix talismans to the bands as though they were light armor.

Activate—Return Force R (force, manipulate)

Trigger A creature critically misses you with a melee Strike

Effect You Shove the creature using the bands' Athletics modifier of [[/r d20+14]]{+14}.

equipment->Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa.name: Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa
equipment->Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa.description:
Functions as a Greater Symbol of Conflict

This tarnished necklace can be attuned only by someone who is holy or unholy. When you attune it, it transforms into your deity's religious symbol or a personal symbol if you don't have a deity. You receive a +2 item bonus to Religion and a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against holy and unholy effects.

Activate—Presence 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect The symbol casts 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.7ZinJNzxq0XF0oMx]{Bane}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.XSujb7EsSwKl19Uu]{Bless}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.hVU9msO9yGkxKZ3J]{Divine Wrath}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.SUKaxVZW2TlM8lu0]{Cleanse Affliction}.

The counteract DC of these effects is 27, and the counteract modifier is +17.

Craft Requirements You must be holy or unholy.

consumable->Potion of Shared Memories.name: Potion of Shared Memories
consumable->Potion of Shared Memories.description:
Activate 1 (manipulate)

A potion of shared memories can transfer recollections from one creature to another. To place a memory in the potion, you must hold the vial and focus on a particular memory for 1 minute. This memory must be of a single event, location, person, or otherwise encompass a span of about 1 minute. The clear fluid takes on a shimmering hue reminiscent of the stored memory and gains a slightly sweet taste.

Upon consuming the potion, the drinker vividly recalls the memory, and thereafter can remember it as easily as a memory they actually experienced. An unwilling drinker can refuse to absorb the memory.

action->Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa.name: Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa
action->Bleeding Heart of Urgathoa.description: Red ichor drips from the heart-shaped warshard embedded in Illcayna's chest. This warshard functions as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Wtm4JdxeTLQi3zQI]{Symbol of Conflict (Greater)}, except it's attuned only to Urgathoa. As long as Illcayna exists, non-Urgathoans can't use the warshard even if it's removed. If Illcayna dies, the warshard acts as a regular greater symbol of conflict.

action->Reaper's Sigh.name: Reaper's Sigh
action->Reaper's Sigh.description: Illcayna is fused with Reaper's Sigh, granting her a +4 circumstance bonus to her Reflex DC against attempts to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} the scythe. This bonus is reduced to +2 for 1 round after she's critically hit with a slashing weapon.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Holy.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Holy Effects

action->Field of Unholy Undeath.name: Field of Unholy Undeath
action->Field of Unholy Undeath.description: 40 feet. Undead allies within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws. If the ally has void healing, they also gain fast healing 5. A living creature who doesn't worship Urgathoa takes @Damage[6d6[void]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:26|basic] save) when they enter or start their turn in the aura. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, that creature becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 day.

action->Life to Unlife.name: Life to Unlife
action->Life to Unlife.description:
Frequency once per minute

Trigger A creature within 30 feet that Illcayna can observe Casts a divine Spell with the healing trait

Effect Illcayna sadistically twists the healing magic against the living. She expends a prepared spell of the same rank as the triggering spell and then attempts to counteract the triggering spell using Religion. If she successfully counteracts the spell, Illcayna consumes the magic and regurgitates it as a 3-action harm spell of the rank of the countered spell.

action->Share Blessings.name: Share Blessings
action->Share Blessings.description: When Illcayna is destroyed, she explodes in a blast of infectious rot. All creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] are exposed to pallid plague.

action->Pallid Plague.name: Pallid Plague
action->Pallid Plague.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:27]

Stage 1 carrier (3 days)

Stage 2 @Damage[3d8[void]] damage and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @Damage[3d8[void]] damage, doomed 1, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 4 @Damage[4d8[void]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 2}, and drained 1 (1 day)

Stage 5 @Damage[4d8[void]] damage, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed 3}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day)

Stage 6 dead.

A creature that dies while infected with pallid plague rises as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.DBTbqI9QQRtlJwWh]{Wight}.

action->Reap.name: Reap
action->Reap.description: Illcayna makes a Reaper's Sigh Strike and compares the attack roll result to the ACs of two foes who must both be within her melee reach. Roll damage only once, and apply it to each creature hit. Reap counts as two attacks for Illcayna's multiple attack penalty.

action->Wake the Dead.name: Wake the Dead
action->Wake the Dead.description:
Frequency once per minute

Requirements Illcayna is adjacent to a creature that died within the last minute

Effect Illcayna animates the dead with a touch of her hand. The creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:29] save or arise as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.DBTbqI9QQRtlJwWh]{Wight}. These wights automatically become loyal to Illcayna and independently act in her interest and follow her command until destroyed.

Insidiator.name: Insidiator
Insidiator.publicNotes:
These devils from the first layer of Hell serve their betters by ensnaring those who are easily tempted and weaving plots that lead to the fall of mortal souls. Insidiators are commonly recruited to set up sting operations that entice mortals into committing terrible sins or lay bare the machinations of fiendish rivals. They delight in seeing their victims fall prey to their wellcrafted schemes and might spend years on the perfect plan, though the most efficient are more likely to rise through Hell's ranks.

An insidiator's talent for subtlety extends to their physical form. Their bat-like wings appear gossamer but, like the spider webs they resemble, are excellent for catching prey, sticking to enemies and trapping them in melee. When the mood strikes or circumstances warrant, entrapment devils can alter the color of their skin and wings to avoid detection.

Insidiators generally seek to avoid physical combat, preferring to toy with their targets in games of subterfuge and mental manipulation. Their specialty is leading a target down a convoluted path that ends with the target incriminating or damning themselves, forcing them to agree to the insidiator's demands. Should a would-be victim see through the insidiator's schemes, the devil concedes temporary defeat and leaves to begin plotting anew. When all else fails and physical tactics become necessary, entrapment devils are capable fighters who use their spiderlike webs and camouflage abilities to their advantage, employing sneak tactics to throw their opponents off-guard.
Insidiator.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Chameleon Skin.name: Chameleon Skin
action->Chameleon Skin.description: The insidiator changes the coloration of their skin to match their surrounding environment. Doing so allows them to Hide even if they don't have cover. When the insidiator moves to a different environment, they must use this action again or lose its effects.

action->Change Shape.name: Change Shape
action->Change Shape.description:
The insidiator can take on the appearance of any Medium humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

@Localize[PF2E.NPC.Abilities.Glossary.ChangeShape]

action->Hidden Movement.name: Hidden Movement
action->Hidden Movement.description: If the insidiator starts their turn undetected or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} to a creature, that creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against the insidiator's attacks until the end of the turn.

action->Web Trap.name: Web Trap
action->Web Trap.description: A creature hit by the insidiator's web Strike is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and stuck to the nearest surface until it [[/act escape dc=17]]{Escapes}.

Instillo.name: Instillo
Instillo.publicNotes: Wherever learning and instruction is called for within the burning shadows of Hell, from the bureaus of Dis and coffers of Erebus to the war colleges of Malebolge and Caina's courtrooms, docent devils ply their unassuming and perspicacious trade. The instillo, so named for their creativity and talent in instilling a respect for Hell's ways and legitimacy to captive audiences, are valued for their ability to transform recently conquered lands into compliant, even loyal, client-states. The conquered are taught to be ashamed of their origins; the despairing are issued philosophies that displace blame and uncertainty upon the mistakes of history and geography; the orphaned are taught to respect those who orphaned them.
Instillo.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

weapon->Light Mace.name: Light Mace
weapon->Light Mace.description: A light mace has a short wooden or metal shaft ending with a dense metal head. Used much like a club, it delivers heavy bludgeoning blows, but with extra power derived from the head's metal ridges or spikes.

equipment->Clothing (Fine).name: Clothing (Fine)
equipment->Clothing (Fine).description: Fine clothing, suitable for a noble or royal, is made with expensive fabrics, precious metals, and intricate patterns.

equipment->Writing Set.name: Writing Set
equipment->Writing Set.description: Using a writing set, you can draft correspondence and scribe scrolls. A set includes stationery, including a variety of paper and parchment, as well as ink, a quill or inkpen, sealing wax, and a simple seal. If you've written a large amount, you can refill your kit with extra ink and paper.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Correcting Veil.name: Correcting Veil
action->Correcting Veil.description: 100 feet. The instillo's telepathic presence constantly monitors perspectives and molds thought toward an infernal clarity. Creatures within the aura gain the Dubious Knowledge skill feat but are unaware of this effect. The instillo is instantly aware when any creature within the aura fails a Recall Knowledge check and influences the erroneous information that the creature gains.

action->Nod of the Tongue.name: Nod of the Tongue
action->Nod of the Tongue.description: Creatures who can understand Diabolic take a –2 status penalty to their Will saves against the instillo's effects with the auditory or mental trait.

action->Quiet Confidence.name: Quiet Confidence
action->Quiet Confidence.description: The instillo can hide the source of their magic. If the next action the instillo uses is to Cast a Spell, that spell gains the subtle trait. The trait hides only the spell's spellcasting actions and manifestations, not its effects.

action->Sneak Attack.name: Sneak Attack
action->Sneak Attack.description: The instillo's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} creatures.

action->Superlative Rhetoric.name: Superlative Rhetoric
action->Superlative Rhetoric.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The instillo's most recent action was to cast a spell with the auditory trait

Effect The instillo immediately casts @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.4gBIw4IDrSfFHik4]{Daze} at a creature that can hear them.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

weapon->Shortbow.name: Shortbow
weapon->Shortbow.description: This smaller bow is made of a single piece of wood and favored by skirmishers and cavalry.

weapon->Shortsword.name: Shortsword
weapon->Shortsword.description: These blades come in a variety of shapes and styles, but they are typically 2 feet long.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

action->Hunt Prey.name: Hunt Prey
action->Hunt Prey.description:
The Isgeri Slayer designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're Tracking, as their prey.

The slayer gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [[/act seek]] the prey and to Survival checks to [[/act track]] the prey.

The first time the slayer hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d8 precision damage. The slayer also ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment. These effects last until the poacher uses Hunt Prey again.

action->Twin Takedown.name: Twin Takedown
action->Twin Takedown.description:
Requirements The Isgeri Slayer is wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand

Effect The Isgeri Slayer swiftly attacks their hunted prey with each of their weapons, potentially combining their damage into a single devastating attack. They make two Strikes against their hunted prey, one with each of the required weapons. If both hit the same hunted prey, combine their damage for the purpose of its resistances and weaknesses. The Isgeri Slayer applies their multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Full Plate.name: Full Plate
armor->Full Plate.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.zBYEU9E7034ENCmh]{Padded Armor} and a pair of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Ix2vicchE79d6Cl3]{gauntlets}.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Bravery.name: Bravery
action->Bravery.description: When Jordis rolls a success on a Will save against a fear effect, they get a critical success instead. Any time they gain the frightened condition, reduce its value by 1.

action->Shield Warden.name: Shield Warden
action->Shield Warden.description: When Jordis has their shield raised, they can Shield Block when an attack is made against an adjacent ally. If they do, the shield prevents that ally from taking damage instead of Jordis.

action->Shielded Advance.name: Shielded Advance
action->Shielded Advance.description:
Requirements Jordis has their shield raised

Effect Jordis presses forward, using their shield to push back foes. Jordis Strides and Shoves, in either order. The multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove}, though the Shove does count toward Jordis's multiple attack penalty.

Kylix.name: Kylix
Kylix.publicNotes:
The elegant and mortal-appearing kylixes, or cupbearer devils, rarely hide their infernal nature, preferring to dress tastefully and immaculately to accentuate the beauty and symmetry of their forms. Theirs is the dance of idle speech and clever chatter, half-promises of friendship amid smiling hints of dalliance. They make friends swiftly wherever they go, and their presence alone seems to prevent insurrection and conflict. The cupbearers are the face of occupation; with their devilish charms, they ca

The cupbearers shape art and culture, wielding their talents in hospitality and merrymaking to seduce their guests and entourages toward the glamour of evil. Hell is, after all, a bastion of civilization; the cupbearers, with their twinkling vessels and carelessly sincere flattery, remind that Hell is the best place to be, and you have nothing to fear from its laws.
Kylix.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic
Kylix.languagesDetails: plus three regional languages; telepathy 100 feet

equipment->Clothing (Fine).name: Clothing (Fine)
equipment->Clothing (Fine).description: Fine clothing, suitable for a noble or royal, is made with expensive fabrics, precious metals, and intricate patterns.

action->False Friend.name: False Friend
action->False Friend.description: The kylix's non-damaging spells gain the subtle trait.

action->Raise a Glass.name: Raise a Glass
action->Raise a Glass.description: When a kylix is holding a drink and not wielding weapons or wearing armor, they're continually protected by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.8xRzLhwGL7Dgy3EZ]{Sanctuary} spell (@Check[will|dc:21]). If the kylix breaks the sanctuary spell, the effect returns if the devil doesn't take any hostile actions for 10 minutes. Additionally, while protected by a sanctuary spell, the kylix can roll Diplomacy for initiative.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Courteous Causerie.name: Courteous Causerie
action->Courteous Causerie.description: The kylix attempts a @Check[deception], @Check[diplomacy], @Check[performance], or @Check[society] check, and compares their result against the Will DCs of up to four creatures within 60 feet. On a success, these creatures are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} for 1 round. The kylix can Sustain this effect, increasing the duration of this fascination by 1 round for up to 1 minute. When the fascination ends, the affected creatures are then temporarily immune to Courteous Causerie for 1 hour.

action->Hand in Glove.name: Hand in Glove
action->Hand in Glove.description: The kylix can flank an enemy if it's within reach of both an ally and themself; the kylix and their ally don't have to be on opposite sides. This benefits the kylix's allies as well, but only if they're flanking with the kylix, not each other. Other requirements for flanking must still be met.

action->Sparkling Wit.name: Sparkling Wit
action->Sparkling Wit.description: A creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AdPVz7rbaVSRxHFg]{Fascinated} by a kylix is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the kylix.

weapon->Sickle.name: Sickle
weapon->Sickle.description: Originally a farming tool used for reaping grain, this one-handed weapon has a short wooden handle ending in a curved blade, sometimes sharpened on both sides.

weapon->Lance.name: Lance
weapon->Lance.description: This spear-like weapon is used by a mounted creature to deal a great deal of damage.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

action->Denounce.name: Denounce
action->Denounce.description:
Lefranek condemns an enemy within 30 feet as a Chelaxian dog and enemy of Isger. Until the start of Lefranek's next turn, any Strikes she or her allies make against the creature do an additional 1d4 precision damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.izuJvgHjqI6kQNbK]{Effect: Denounce}

action->False Flag.name: False Flag
action->False Flag.description: 60 feet. Lefranek's false flag creates panic and confusion. Hellbreakers in the area take a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws and DCs vs. effects that give the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused} condition. Add 1d8 to the Gala Crowd hazard's check to determine its action during its routine.

Lights Out.name: Lights Out
Lights Out.descriptionHazard: Patches of darkness envelop the rickety beds in this room, from which can be heard the sounds of gentle snoring from children.
Lights Out.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:18] (trained) to ward off the darkness or @Check[deception|dc:19] or @Check[performance|dc:19] (trained) to pretend to be asleep; two total success are required to disable the haunt
Lights Out.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all living creatures leave the room but resets immediately thereafter. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer here is destroyed.
Lights Out.routine: (1 action; darkness, occult) The haunt randomly chooses 1 living creature that it hadn't engulfed in darkness the previous round. Supernatural darkness wells over that creature, who must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. On a failure, that creature is engulfed in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] of darkness centered on them and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} for 1 round. Any creatures in the emanation must attempt their own Will save or become blinded as well. Any non-magical light sources and magical light of 1st rank or lower the primary creature is holding or wearing cease to give off light for that duration.

action->Lullaby and Good Night.name: Lullaby and Good Night
action->Lullaby and Good Night.description:
Trigger At least two living creatures enter the room

Effect The sounds of a soothing lullaby fill the air. All living creatures in the room must attempt a @Check[will|dc:22] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature gets slightly drowsy, taking a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 1 round.

Failure The creature briefly nods off. They are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions for 1 round after that.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2}.

Littler Beasts.name: Littler Beasts
Littler Beasts.descriptionHazard: At first, the tree looks small. Then the space around it warps to give the impression that it's a normalsized tree seen at a distance.
Littler Beasts.disable: @Check[nature|dc:20] or @Check[occultism|dc:20] twice to disperse the warped magic around the tree, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (2nd rank; counteract DC 19) to counteract the warped magic
Littler Beasts.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 60 feet of it at the start of its turn.
Littler Beasts.routine: (1 action) The trap targets a random creature within 60 feet to try to bring it down to its size. That creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. On a failure, the creature becomes Tiny in size until the beginning of its next turn. An affected creature's equipment shrinks with it but returns to its original size if removed. The creature's reach changes to 0 feet. This is a polymorph effect that has no effect on a Tiny creature. On a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for the same duration.

action->Action.name: Action
action->Action.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 30 feet of the tree

Effect The tree seems to grow and shrink simultaneously. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must attempt a @Check[will|dc:21] saving throw. The trap then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} until the end of its next turn from the disorientation.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} as if unsure of the distance between places.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 2}.

weapon->Whip.name: Whip
weapon->Whip.description: This long strand of thick leather, often braided, delivers a painful but nonlethal slash at a distance, usually accompanied by a distinctive cracking sound.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).name: Sturdy Shield (Minor)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 64, and BT 32.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

action->+3 Status to Reflex Saves vs. Ar.name: +3 Status to Reflex Saves vs. Area Effects

action->Disillusionment.name: Disillusionment
action->Disillusionment.description: Whenever Nayren succeeds on a save against an illusion effect, they get a critical success instead.

action->Return to Battle.name: Return to Battle
action->Return to Battle.description:
Trigger Nayren reaches 0 Hit Points or fewer

Effect The battle within the nephilim's blood runs on. Nayren automatically stabilizes and gains 1 Hit Point. They Stand. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

action->Rack the Dissidents.name: Rack the Dissidents
action->Rack the Dissidents.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Nayren's strikes keeps them empowered to continue to take down rebels. For 1 minute or until they critically miss, the first time each round Nayren successfully Strikes a creature, they gain 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round.

action->Silence, Heretic!.name: Silence, Heretic!
action->Silence, Heretic!.description: Nayren makes a longsword Strike. If the attack hits, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:29] save or be unable to use auditory actions for 1 round. This also prevents the target from casting spells, with the exception of subtle spells or spells that don't require the use of magical words in casting. On a critical failure, the duration is instead 1 minute. Regardless of the outcome of the save, the target becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 10 minutes.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Morningstar.name: Morningstar
weapon->Morningstar.description: This weapon has a short shaft ending in a metal ball studded with spikes.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

HardnessHPBT
52010

armor->Breastplate.name: Breastplate
armor->Breastplate.description: Though referred to as a breastplate, this type of armor consists of several pieces of plate or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.pRoikbRo5HFW6YUB]{Half Plate} armor that protect the torso, chest, neck, and sometimes the hips and lower legs. It strategically grants some of the protection of plate while allowing greater flexibility and speed.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

weapon->Dart.name: Dart
weapon->Dart.description: This thrown weapon is larger than an arrow but shorter than a javelin. It typically has a short shaft of wood ending in a metal tip and is sometimes stabilized by feathers or fur.

action->Flurry of Blows.name: Flurry of Blows
action->Flurry of Blows.description: The novitiate makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

Odvolos.name: Odvolos
Odvolos.publicNotes: As empires posture amid decline and demagogues preside over republics, censorship devils scatter the burning ash of ignorance as a comforting veil over the complications of history and causality. These devils are feverishly devoted to Hell and find anything that could be interpreted against Hell's doctrines to be anathema and deserving of immediate destruction. Inconvenient accounts of the past, truths that draw the faithful away from the ways of Asmodeus, words that bring hope to those who might otherwise fall into a devil's temptations—these are fuel for the odvolos's hateful torches, burning bright on the cinders of ink and vellum.
Odvolos.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all save vs. magic, -1 to all saves vs. emotion effects
Odvolos.sensesDetails: (+14 to detect concealed objects); fevered consciousness

spell->Locate (At Will, Books, Scrolls,.name: Locate (At Will, Books, Scrolls, Ink, Writing Materials, and Other Written or Inscribed Materials Only)

weapon->Longspear.name: Longspear
weapon->Longspear.description: This very long spear, sometimes called a pike, is purely for thrusting rather than throwing. Used by many soldiers and city watch for crowd control and defense against charging enemies, it must be wielded with two hands.

condition->Concealed.name: Concealed
condition->Concealed.description: You are difficult for one or more creatures to see due to thick fog or some other obscuring feature. You can be concealed to some creatures but not others. While concealed, you can still be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.1wQY3JYyhMYeeV2G]{Observed}, but you're tougher to target. A creature that you're concealed from must succeed at a @Check[flat|showDC:all|dc:5] when targeting you with an attack, spell, or other effect. If the check fails, you aren't affected. Area effects aren't subject to this flat check.

effect->Effect: Shroud of Burning Pages.name: Effect: Shroud of Burning Pages
effect->Effect: Shroud of Burning Pages.description: The odvolos is surrounded by an aura of burning paper and scorched parchment, which provides concealment as if it was smoke. A creature within the aura takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks, and a creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or any items with written information held or carried by the creature takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage. Magical items and religious texts are immune to this effect.

action->Fevered Consciousness.name: Fevered Consciousness
action->Fevered Consciousness.description: An odvolos ignores @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealment} or penalties to Perception from ash, fire, and smoke.

action->-1 Status to All Saves vs. Emoti.name: -1 Status to All Saves vs. Emotion Effects

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Purge Records.name: Purge Records
action->Purge Records.description:
Trigger A creature the odvolos can see or hear within 15 feet Casts a Spell from a scroll or Activates a grimoire

Effect The odvolos Steps toward the creature and makes a longspear Strike. If the attack is a critical hit, the triggering action is disrupted.

action->Shroud of Burning Pages.name: Shroud of Burning Pages
action->Shroud of Burning Pages.description: 15 feet. The odvolos is surrounded by an aura of burning paper and scorched parchment, which provides concealment as if it was smoke. A creature within the aura takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks, and a creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:22] save or any items with written information held or carried by the creature takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,fire]] damage. Magical items and religious texts are immune to this effect.

action->Pyre's Mercy.name: Pyre's Mercy
action->Pyre's Mercy.description:
The odvolos attempts to [[/act demoralize]] a creature within 30 feet. If the odvolos succeeds, the creature also gains fire weakness 5 while frightened.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.u3Qy9t7RuE4wKV0O]{Effect: Pyre's Mercy}

action->Torchbearer.name: Torchbearer
action->Torchbearer.description: Piercing weapons wielded by an odvolos gain the magical trait and deal an additional 1d4 dire damage on a successful Strike and 1d8 persistent fire damage on a critical hit.

weapon->Meteor Hammer.name: Meteor Hammer
weapon->Meteor Hammer.description: This weapon consists of a long chain connected to a heavy weight at each end. When a wielder swings the weights by the chain, they build momentum and can serve as deadly bludgeons with incredible reach.

armor->Chain Mail.name: Chain Mail
armor->Chain Mail.description: A suit of chain mail consists of several pieces of armor composed of small metal rings linked together in a protective mesh. It typically includes a chain shirt, leggings, a pair of arms, and a coif, collectively protecting most of the body.

equipment->Waterskin.name: Waterskin
equipment->Waterskin.description: When it's full, a waterskin contains roughly 1 day's worth of water for a Small or Medium creature.

equipment->Flint and Steel.name: Flint and Steel
equipment->Flint and Steel.description: Flint and steel are useful in creating a fire if you have the time to catch a spark, though using them is typically too time-consuming to be practical during an encounter. Even in ideal conditions, using flint and steel to light a flame requires using at least 3 actions, and often significantly longer.

equipment->Torch.name: Torch
equipment->Torch.description: A torch sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) for 1 hour. It can be used as an improvised weapon that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage plus 1 fire damage.

backpack->Backpack.name: Backpack
backpack->Backpack.description: A backpack holds up to 4 Bulk of items and the first 2 Bulk of these items don't count against your Bulk limits. If you're carrying or stowing the pack rather than wearing it on your back, its Bulk is light instead of negligible

action->Warfare Expertise.name: Warfare Expertise
action->Warfare Expertise.description: Phoebe can use Warfare Lore to Recall Knowledge about most creatures regardless of type, but typically only to determine whether they can be reasoned with, their most notable offensive abilities, and whether one of their saving throws is particularly weak.

action->Banner Bearer.name: Banner Bearer
action->Banner Bearer.description: 30 feet. As Phoebe displays the Hellbreakers League banner, all allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to saves and DCs against devils and Hellknights.

action->Commander's Orders.name: Commander's Orders
action->Commander's Orders.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Phoebe calls out one of the following orders to all her allies in her Hellbreakers League banner aura, letting them immediately take the associated action as a reaction.

  • On Guard! The ally can Raise a Shield, defensively position a weapon with the parry trait, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ust1jJSCZQUhBZIz]{Take Cover}. If the ally isn't benefiting from cover or near a feature that allows them to Take Cover, they can fall @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} as part of the reaction.
  • Send Them Back to Hell An ally who's flanking a devil, a creature that worships devils, or a creature that has a magical contract with a devil can immediately attempt a Strike.
  • To Me! The ally can Stride but must end their movement in the aura. The ally can Burrow, Climb, Fly, or Swim instead of Striding if they have the corresponding movement type.

action->Sense Heartbond.name: Sense Heartbond
action->Sense Heartbond.description:
Frequency once per day

Effect Phoebe learns Alceo's present state. She knows his direction and distance, and any conditions affecting him.

action->Trained Reaction.name: Trained Reaction
action->Trained Reaction.description:
Requirements Phoebe uses Commander's Orders

Effect Phoebe grants one ally in her Hellbreakers League banner aura an extra reaction that they can use only to follow her Commander's Orders. If they don't use the reaction, it's lost.

Play Time.name: Play Time
Play Time.descriptionHazard: An old, dusty toy box creaks open and forcefully expels a series of cracked and broken toys.
Play Time.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:19] (trained) to exorcise the spirits of the toy box, @Check[athletics|dc:20] (trained) to hold the toy box closed momentarily, or @Check[diplomacy|dc:21] (trained) to convince the spirits that play time is over; three total success are required to disable the haunt
Play Time.reset: The haunt deactivates 1 minute after all creatures leave the room or if the toy box is destroyed. After 1 hour, if the toy box remains, the haunt reactivates. If disabled, the haunt resets after 1 day. The haunt is permanently destroyed when the attic whisperer in area D5 is destroyed.
Play Time.routine: (1 action) The toy box belches forth a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] of toys, dealing 3d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (chosen randomly each time) with a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic] save.

action->Terrifying Toy Box.name: Terrifying Toy Box
action->Terrifying Toy Box.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 10 feet of the toy box

Effect The toy box lid slowly opens with a groan, revealing a yawning chasm into nothingness. Each creature who can see the toy box must attempt a @Check[will|dc:23] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 while the haunt is active.

Critical Failure As failure, but @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4}.

Practice Guard's Grasp.name: Practice Guard's Grasp
Practice Guard's Grasp.descriptionHazard: A spring-loaded net fires from the dummy, then is pulled tightly back in with magnets.
Practice Guard's Grasp.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:18] (trained) to jam the net or @Check[athletics|dc:25] to knock the dummy over

action->Got You!.name: Got You!
action->Got You!.description:
Trigger A creature moves adjacent to the trap

Effect The creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:23] save or be @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} on a critical failure). The DC to Escape is [[/act escape dc=23]], or the net can be cut by doing 6 acid, fire, or slashing damage to it.

Prison Break.name: Prison Break
Prison Break.descriptionHazard: A prison mob makes a break for freedom, trampling anything in its path.
Prison Break.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:27] to stop the prisoners in their tracks, @Check[diplomacy|dc:30] to reason with the mob, or @Check[intimidation|dc:27] to cow them into calming down
Prison Break.routine:
(1 action) Prisoners trample toward the exit, dealing @Damage[(2d10+9)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each living creature in area K9 and the hallway north of it (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature's grasp on anything it's carrying is weakened as if the hazard succeeded on a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} attempt. If its grasp was already weakened, they drop the item.

Special If the PCs have not defeated the hazard after 4 rounds, it automatically ends as all the prisoners escape.

action->Rush to Freedom.name: Rush to Freedom
action->Rush to Freedom.description:
Trigger The door to holding cell B opens

Effect The hazard grabs at the weapons of any creatures within 20 feet of the door, attempting to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Dt6B1slsBy8ipJu9]{Disarm} them with a +19 modifier, and then rolls initiative.

Ravenous Pit.name: Ravenous Pit
Ravenous Pit.descriptionHazard: A seemingly ordinary hole in the forest floor exerts a strange gravity on creatures that come near.
Ravenous Pit.disable: @Check[nature|dc:23] or @Check[occultism|dc:23] (expert) three times to disperse the unusual energy surrounding the hole, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the engulfing magic
Ravenous Pit.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures within 20 feet of it at the start of its turn.
Ravenous Pit.routine:
(2 actions) For each action, the pit chooses a different creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] to attempt to swallow. That creature must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:22] save. On a failure, the creature is pulled 5 feet closer to the hole (10 feet on a critical failure). If this would pull the creature into the pit, that creature can attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.3yoajuKjwHZ9ApUY]{Grab an Edge} as a reaction (DC 22). A creature who falls into the pit disappears into an extradimensional space that feels like being buried alive. A creature takes @Damage[(2d8+7)[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save) at the end of each its turns in the extradimensional space. A creature in this extradimensional space is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1}; they can @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} this extradimensional space with a successful @Check[athletics|dc:22], Nature, or Survival check. If they succeed, they appear in a random spot no more than 10 feet from the hole as if they had clawed their way from the dirt.

While this trap is active, all creatures treat movement away from the pit as difficult terrain.

action->Deadly Inhale.name: Deadly Inhale
action->Deadly Inhale.description:
Trigger A creature approaches within 15 feet of the hole

Effect The ravenous pit seems to "inhale," attempting to pull creatures into itself. Each creature within 15 feet must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:24] save. On a failure, the creature is pulled 5 feet closer to the hole (10 feet on a critical failure). The pit then rolls initiative.

Redcap.name: Redcap
Redcap.publicNotes:
Redcaps are sadistic and capricious fey who thrill in bloodletting and murder. While they are most famous for appearing as wizened, bearded men, redcaps of other genders are no less fearsome or cruel. However, redcaps are ultimately craven bullies, cowed by anything more powerful than themselves, a trait that leads them to fear and despise the symbols of deities.

Many fairy tales explain how the redcaps draw power from dipping the hats for which they are named in fresh blood. Just as iconic to these cruel little fey are their iron-clad boots, and the clanging sound of their metal soles clanking on stone floors is both discordant and disconcerting—especially to those who recognize the sound for what it is. Redcaps typically stand only 3 feet tall, with hunched frames, pointed ears, crooked teeth, and long, white, tangled hair.

Though unlikely to share true affection, redcaps find camaraderie in murder. Small troupes of redcaps travel together, sharing bloody kills and reminiscing on their brutal exploits. They loathe the company of most other creatures, with the notable exception of the feline fey called elananxes.

weapon->Halberd.name: Halberd
weapon->Halberd.description: This polearm has a relatively short, 5-foot shaft. The business end is a long spike with an axe blade attached.

action->Red Cap.name: Red Cap
action->Red Cap.description:
A redcap's woolen hat is dyed with the blood of their victims. If the redcap loses their cap, they no longer benefit from fast healing and take a –4 status penalty to their damage rolls. They can create a new cap in 10 minutes, but that cap doesn't grant them powers until the redcap has turned it red with Blood Soak. A cap has no benefit for creatures other than the redcap who made it.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.s5mS7CyE0oOYlecv]{Effect: Lost Red Cap}

action->Divine Revulsion.name: Divine Revulsion
action->Divine Revulsion.description:
If a redcap sees a creature brandish a religious symbol of a deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or cast a divine spell while wearing a religious symbol, the redcap must attempt a @Check[will|dc:19] save. They then become temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The redcap is unaffected.

Success The redcap is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The redcap gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} condition for 1 round and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 4}.

action->Blood Soak.name: Blood Soak
action->Blood Soak.description:
The redcap dips their cap in the blood of a slain foe. The foe must have died in the last minute, and the redcap must have helped kill it. The redcap gains a +4 status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.wPIGBcpCqCWgrCiq]{Effect: Blood Soak}

action->Deadly Cleave.name: Deadly Cleave
action->Deadly Cleave.description:
Trigger The redcap reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with a halberd Strike

Effect The redcap makes another halberd Strike against a different creature, using the same multiple attack penalty as the halberd Strike that triggered this reaction. This counts toward their multiple attack penalty as normal.

action->Red Drip.name: Red Drip
action->Red Drip.description:
Whenever the redcap successfully Strikes a creature, that creature must attempt a Fortitude save against this debilitating fungal infection that slowly consumes a limb

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:22]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}, and using the infected limb deals @Damage[3d6[persistent,bleed]] damage (1 day)

Stage 3 clumsy 2, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}, and the infected limb is unusable (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 3}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3}, and infected limb is permanently unusable (1 day)

Stage 5 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.yblD8fOR1J8rDwEQ]{Confused}, and damage does not end the confused condition (1 day)

Stage 6 death

action->Stomp.name: Stomp
action->Stomp.description: The redcap Strides up to half its Speed and makes a boot Strike at any point during that movement. If the boot Strike hits a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} creature, it deals an extra @Damage[2d6[bleed]].

Ritual Backlash.name: Ritual Backlash
Ritual Backlash.descriptionHazard: The space above the flame in the ivory bowl wavers and the smell of brimstone assaults the senses.
Ritual Backlash.disable: @Check[occultism|dc:20] or @Check[religion|dc:20] three times to suppress the backlash of energy, or defeat Viro Ahala
Ritual Backlash.reset: Once Viro Ahala has been defeated, this trap can't be reset.
Ritual Backlash.routine: (3 actions) The hazard makes a ranged mindburn Strike, creates a Pulse of Fear, and bestows Vitality of Hell to Viro or one of his allies at random. If it's reduced to 2 actions, the hazard doesn't create a Pulse of Fear. If reduced to 1 action, the hazard only bestows Vitality of Hell.

action->Pulse of Fear.name: Pulse of Fear
action->Pulse of Fear.description: Each of Viro's enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:20] from the ivory bowl must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:18] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} on a critical failure).

action->Vitality of Hell.name: Vitality of Hell
action->Vitality of Hell.description:
Viro or one of his allies gains 1d6 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.bwEZazVhE5KkLfwA]{Effect: Vitality of Hell}

action->Lash Out.name: Lash Out
action->Lash Out.description:
Trigger Viro Ahala rolls initiative

Effect Viro's lack of full attention unleashes the latent magical energy of the cave. The hazard creates a Pulse of Fear and then rolls initiative.

Sarcovalt.name: Sarcovalt
Sarcovalt.publicNotes:
Individual sarcovalts have almost no personal identities and incredibly short-term memories.

There are few creatures throughout the multiverse more bizarrely revolting than the sarcovalt, a fiend with the body of a fly and the head of a fleshless vulture skull. While a creature barely 2 feet long might not seem threatening, sarcovalt swarms can overwhelm entire villages. Sarcovalts' heads almost seem to be wholly separate entities from their bodies and will vomit up whatever they capture in their beaks hours later as putrid, coagulated sludge.
Sarcovalt.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. death effects

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Death.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Death Effects

action->Aura of Blight.name: Aura of Blight
action->Aura of Blight.description:
15 feet. The sarcovalt is surrounded by a miasma of infection. An enemy that enters or ends their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 minute.

Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against disease effects.

Critical Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –2 circumstance bonus against disease effects and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}.

action->Befouled Body.name: Befouled Body
action->Befouled Body.description:
Any water the sarcovalt touches becomes diseased with filth fever.

Filth Fever (disease)

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:23|traits:disease]

Onset [[/br 1d4 #days]]{1d4 days}

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day)

action->Reactive Detach.name: Reactive Detach
action->Reactive Detach.description:
Trigger The sarcovalt takes slashing damage from a creature while they have another creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect The sarcovalt uses Detach Head. Their head keeps the other creature grabbed or restrained, and their body Steps. The sarcovalt gains resistance 5 to the triggering slashing damage.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.78Dfh5ghvULGiyzk]{Effect: Reactive Detach}

action->Return to Sender.name: Return to Sender
action->Return to Sender.description:
Trigger A creature escapes from being @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained} by the sarcovalt's detached head

Effect The sarcovalt's head returns to their body via the shortest possible path. Any creature in a straight line between the head and the body must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:21] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical failure). The sarcovalt Dismisses Detach Head.

action->Detach Head.name: Detach Head
action->Detach Head.description:
Trigger The sarcovalt takes damage from a critical hit with a slashing weapon or has their head removed (such as by a vorpal weapon)

Effect The sarcovalt can survive decapitation and isn't killed if an effect that removes their head would kill them. Instead, the sarcovalt's head becomes detached from their body. The detached head has the same statistics as the body (except for the aura of blight) and gains the minion trait. The sarcovalt's body can issue a verbal command to the detached head as a single action, giving the head 2 actions. The detached head and body share the same pool of Hit Points.

While their head is detached, the sarcovalt's body can't make beak Strikes or use Malodorous Shriek. The detached head can only Climb, Fly, Stride, make beak Strikes, or use Malodorous Shriek.

action->Malodorous Shriek.name: Malodorous Shriek
action->Malodorous Shriek.description: The sarcovalt screeches through their skull (detached or not), spraying whatever festering viscera remains in their head in a @Template[type:cone|distance:20]. Each creature in the area takes @Damage[3d6[sonic],3d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). The sarcovalt can't use this ability again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge this ability]]{1d4 rounds}.

Sarcovalt Swarm.name: Sarcovalt Swarm
Sarcovalt Swarm.publicNotes:
In most cases, it's very unlikely that one will come across a sarcovalt on their own. More likely, a swarm of Urgathoa's disgusting creations will be beset upon an unfortunate village, the creatures just clever enough to duck away from the best attempts to kill them.

There are few creatures throughout the multiverse more bizarrely revolting than the sarcovalt, a fiend with the body of a fly and the head of a fleshless vulture skull. While a creature barely 2 feet long might not seem threatening, sarcovalt swarms can overwhelm entire villages. Sarcovalts' heads almost seem to be wholly separate entities from their bodies and will vomit up whatever they capture in their beaks hours later as putrid, coagulated sludge.
Sarcovalt Swarm.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. death effects

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Death.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Death Effects

action->Aura of Blight.name: Aura of Blight
action->Aura of Blight.description:
15 feet. The sarcovalt is surrounded by a miasma of infection. An enemy that enters or ends their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:25] save.

Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the aura for 1 minute.

Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against disease effects.

Critical Failure Until the end of its next turn, the creature takes a –2 circumstance bonus against disease effects and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2}.

action->Droning Waste.name: Droning Waste
action->Droning Waste.description: The buzzing of the swarm's wings hit an unholy pitch, making those who hear it feel dizzy and lightheaded. Each creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:15] must attempt a @Check[will|dc:27] save. On a failure, it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls until the start of its next turn (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).

action->Heads Will Roll.name: Heads Will Roll
action->Heads Will Roll.description: The swarm detaches its heads from its bodies and rains them down on its enemies in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] radius within 30 feet. Each head deals falling damage, as if falling from 20 feet up, on any creature it lands on and otherwise takes the damage itself. If a head has a creature within its reach after it falls, it attempts to bite that creature. If any of these bites are critical successes, the head can immediately attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple} the creature.

action->Swarming Bites.name: Swarming Bites
action->Swarming Bites.description: Each enemy in the swarm's space takes @Damage[4d6[piercing],2d4[persistent,bleed]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:27|basic] save).

weapon->Staff.name: Staff
weapon->Staff.description: This long piece of wood can aid in walking and deliver a mighty blow.

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

equipment->Mage's Hat.name: Mage's Hat
equipment->Mage's Hat.description: This hat comes in many forms, such as a colorful turban or a pointy hat with a brim, and is adorned with symbols or runes. It grants you a +1 item bonus to Arcana checks and allows you to cast the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Qw3fnUlaUbnn7ipC]{Prestidigitation} cantrip as an arcane innate cantrip.

equipment->Religious Symbol (Asmodeus).name: Religious Symbol (Asmodeus)
equipment->Religious Symbol (Asmodeus).description: This piece of wood or silver is emblazoned with an image representing a deity. Some divine spellcasters, such as clerics, can use a religious symbol of their deity to use certain abilities and cast some spells. A religious symbol can be worn on the body on a chain or pin, or can be held.

action->Counterspell.name: Counterspell
action->Counterspell.description:
Trigger A creature Casts a Spell Sephone has prepared

Effect Sephone expends a prepared spell, losing the spell slot as if she had cast the triggering spell. She then attempts to counteract the triggering spell.

action->Channel Hellfire.name: Channel Hellfire
action->Channel Hellfire.description:
Frequency three times per day

Effect Sephone fuels her spell with the essence of Hell. The next spell she casts deals fire damage instead of its usual damage type. Any creature that takes damage from the spell and doesn't have the unholy trait also takes an additional @Damage[2d8[spirit]] damage (@Check[fortitude|dc:25|basic] save).

action->Drain Religious Symbol.name: Drain Religious Symbol
action->Drain Religious Symbol.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect Sephone drains her religious symbol and regains a spell she has cast. During her current turn, she can cast one spell she prepared today and already cast.

action->Widen Spell.name: Widen Spell
action->Widen Spell.description: If the next action Sephone uses is to Cast a Spell that has an area of a cone or line and does not have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.

weapon->Asp Coil.name: Asp Coil
weapon->Asp Coil.description: The asp coil, named both for its slithering striking style and its usage among Aspis Consortium agents, has two forms. In sword form, it resembles an elegant, oddly balanced sword. However, with a twist of the pommel, the blade splits into a series of segments connected by elaborate metal cables. Agents of the Aspis Consortium have access to this weapon.

weapon->Dart.name: Dart
weapon->Dart.description: This thrown weapon is larger than an arrow but shorter than a javelin. It typically has a short shaft of wood ending in a metal tip and is sometimes stabilized by feathers or fur.

equipment->Robe of the Erinys.name: Robe of the Erinys
equipment->Robe of the Erinys.description:
This exquisite scarlet robe embroidered with golden depictions of fiends and their weapons is fashioned after the garb of the cloistered Sisters of the Golden Erinys. The robe's belt is embroidered to look like a snake, complete with metal fangs.

Activate—Biting Belt 2 (concentrate, manipulate)

Frequency once per day

Effect You remove the robe's belt and snap it at an enemy within your reach. As you do, it briefly animates to bite that foe, dealing @Damage[2d8[poison]] damage with a basic @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save. On a critical failure, the target is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

action->Bite of the Asp Stance.name: Bite of the Asp Stance
action->Bite of the Asp Stance.description:
Requirements Sister Endreah is wielding an asp coil

Effect Sister Endreah is an expert at using her sword in close quarters. Her asp coil loses the reach weapon property, but she can make asp coil Strikes instead of fist Strikes when she uses Flurry of Blows. Claw of pain also applies to these asp coil Strikes when in this stance.

action->Claw of Pain.name: Claw of Pain
action->Claw of Pain.description: When Sister Endreah targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can cause lingering pain. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:21] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,mental]] damage (and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a critical failure).

action->Flurry of Blows.name: Flurry of Blows
action->Flurry of Blows.description: Sister Endreah makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

weapon->Dart.name: Dart
weapon->Dart.description: This thrown weapon is larger than an arrow but shorter than a javelin. It typically has a short shaft of wood ending in a metal tip and is sometimes stabilized by feathers or fur.

action->Claw of Pain.name: Claw of Pain
action->Claw of Pain.description: When the sister targets the same creature with two Strikes from her Flurry of Blows, she can cause lingering pain. If either Strike hits and deals damage, the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:18] save or take @Damage[1d4[persistent,mental]] damage (and become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a critical failure).
