action->Flurry of Blows.name: Flurry of Blows
action->Flurry of Blows.description: The sister makes two fist Strikes, applying her multiple attack penalty as normal. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger.name: Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger
Skeletal Hellknight Bonesinger.publicNotes:
Some undead Hellknights can command the bones of both their own bodies and others, and they primarily use this ability to temporarily strengthen themselves and summon waves of reinforcements. While just as undead as their compatriots, these so-called bonesingers have enough intelligence to join their living counterparts, though they're generally shunned and disavowed.

The bodies of Hellknights occasionally rise to continue their fight. This is just one example of an undead soldier of Hell.

weapon->Warhammer.name: Warhammer
weapon->Warhammer.description: This weapon has a wooden shaft ending in a large, heavy metal head. The head of the hammer might be single-sided or double-sided, but it's always capable of delivering powerful bludgeoning blows.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

action->Call to Arms.name: Call to Arms
action->Call to Arms.description: The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger strikes its rib cage three times, creating a loud, ominous clatter. All mindless skeletons in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:30] must use their reaction to Stride closer to the bonesinger.

action->For the Cause.name: For the Cause
action->For the Cause.description:
Frequency once per day

Requirements The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger has fewer than half their Hit Points remaining

Effect The bonesinger reaches out through the necromantic magic that animates the dead around them and pulls it into themself. The bonesinger chooses up to three mindless skeletons with the minion trait that they control within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10] and destroys them. The bonesinger regains 2d6 Hit Points for each skeleton destroyed.

action->Invoke Reckoning.name: Invoke Reckoning
action->Invoke Reckoning.description:
Frequency once per round

Requirements The skeletal Hellknight bonesinger's last action was to damage an enemy with a melee Strike

Effect The bonesinger pulls at the bones of the creature they just damaged, releasing a pulse of void energy. That creature takes @Damage[4d8[void]] damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes full damage and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}.

Skirmancer Mage.name: Skirmancer Mage
Skirmancer Mage.publicNotes:
A watchmage uses a mix of magic and martial training to enforce the law. They magically detect invisible criminals, locate stolen property, and counter illegal spells.

Larger societies rely on those with the authority and the ability to interpret and enforce laws. Some carry out these duties fairly, but others are harsh and cruel, imposing severe punishments on anyone unable to pay for clemency.

weapon->Shortbow.name: Shortbow
weapon->Shortbow.description: This smaller bow is made of a single piece of wood and favored by skirmishers and cavalry.

armor->Leather Armor.name: Leather Armor
armor->Leather Armor.description: A mix of flexible and molded boiled leather, a suit of this type of armor provides some protection with maximum flexibility.

equipment->Spellbook.name: Spellbook
equipment->Spellbook.description:
Contains all prepared spells plus @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gPvtmKMRpg9I9D7H]{Earthbind}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.0qaqksrGGDj74HXE]{Revealing Light}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.o4lRVTwSxnOOn5vl]{Sleep}, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.KcLVELhCUcKXxiKE]{Tailwind}.

A spellbook holds the written knowledge necessary to learn and prepare various spells, a necessity for wizards (who typically get one for free) and a useful luxury for other spellcasters looking to learn additional spells. Each spellbook can hold up to 100 spells. The Price listed is for a blank spellbook.

ammo->Arrows.name: Arrows
ammo->Arrows.description: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.

action->Arcane Watch.name: Arcane Watch
action->Arcane Watch.description: The watchmage can either @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.EwgTZBWsc8qKaViP]{Investigate} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.TiNDYUGlMmxzxBYU]{Search} while using the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Yb0C1uLzeHrVLl7a]{Detect Magic} exploration activity.

action->Counter Escape.name: Counter Escape
action->Counter Escape.description:
Trigger A creature Casts a Spell with the teleportation trait or as a reaction

Effect The watchmage expends a spell slot of the same rank or higher as the trigger creature's spell and attempts to counteract the triggering spell (counteract modifier [[/r 1d20+11 #Counteract]]{+11}).

action->Eldritch Arms.name: Eldritch Arms
action->Eldritch Arms.description: In a brief ritual that takes 10 minutes, the watchmage chooses a single weapon or unarmed attack through which they can focus their magic. Strikes the watchmage makes with that weapon are magical and deal 1d6 additional force damage.

action->Spellbound Strike.name: Spellbound Strike
action->Spellbound Strike.description:
Requirements The watchmage is wielding the weapon chosen with Eldritch Arms

Effect The watchmage Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast, imbuing that spell into the weapon. The watchmage Strikes with the required weapon. This counts as two attacks for the watchmage's multiple attack penalty. On a hit, the target is also affected by the spell, though the target gets any normal defenses allowed by the spell.

If the spell is targeted, it targets the creature that was hit and no one else. If the spell is an area, the target must be in the area. A burst is centered on a corner of the target's square if the target is Medium or smaller, or the corner of a square closest to the creature's center if it's Large or larger. A cone or line emits from a square of the watchmage's choice adjacent to the target.

Slashing Rune.name: Slashing Rune
Slashing Rune.descriptionHazard: An invisible rune creates knives of force if someone tries to bypass the lock.
Slashing Rune.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:25] (trained) to silently erase the rune, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (3rd rank; counteract DC 22) to counteract the rune

action->Slash.name: Slash
action->Slash.description:
Trigger A creature attempts a Thievery check to Pick the Lock

Effect Knives of force deal @Damage[(4d8+18)[slashing]] damage to the triggering creature (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a failure, the creature also takes a –1 penalty to Dex-based skills for 1 hour.

Spore Spears.name: Spore Spears
Spore Spears.descriptionHazard: A large patch of moss ripples like the surface of a pond as spears of green plant matter jut outward.
Spore Spears.disable: @Check[nature|dc:17] or @Check[occultism|dc:17] twice to cut off the unstable connection to the Plane of Wood, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.9HpwDN4MYQJnW0LG]{Dispel Magic} (2nd rank; counteract DC 16) to counteract the planar magic
Spore Spears.reset: If the trap isn't disabled, it becomes dormant for 10 minutes if there are no creatures on or within 10 feet of the moss patch at the start of its turn.
Spore Spears.routine: (2 actions) For each action, the moss pond makes a spore spear Strike against a random non-plant creature on or within 10 feet of the moss pond.

action->Planar Ripple.name: Planar Ripple
action->Planar Ripple.description:
Trigger Two or more non-plant creatures step onto the moss pond

Effect The moss pond ripples oddly as its connection to the Plane of Wood intensifies. Each creature on the pond must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:17] save or be knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. A creature who critically fails the save also takes @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] damage from the fall. The pond then rolls initiative.

Spreading Fire.name: Spreading Fire
Spreading Fire.descriptionHazard: A fire engulfs a 5-foot-by-5-foot area, then spreads on each of its turns.
Spreading Fire.disable: @Check[athletics|dc:17] to splash the barrels of gall ink on the fire or @Check[survival|dc:15] to smother the flames
Spreading Fire.routine: (1 action) On its turn, the fire spreads into a number of additional squares equal to half the number of squares the fire currently occupies, with a minimum of 1 square. You determine the squares the fire spreads into—typically those with the most flammable materials. Any creature that ends its turn next to the flames takes @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage, and any creature within the flames takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage. Both of these have a @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save. A creature can take damage from flames only once per round.

action->Spreading Flames.name: Spreading Flames
action->Spreading Flames.description:
Trigger A creature enters the printing house

Effect The flames swell at the influx of air and deal @Damage[1d6[fire]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save). The fire then rolls initiative.

action->Pacify Fey.name: Pacify Fey
action->Pacify Fey.description: 40 feet. All creatures with the fey trait who enter or begin their turn in the aura must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature becomes immune to the aura for 1 day. On a failure, the creature can't take hostile actions against the katroome.

action->Enveloping Web.name: Enveloping Web
action->Enveloping Web.description: The web of the empress katroome wraps creatures into a cocoon. The target becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} while it's enveloped in a cocoon ([[/act escape dc=26]]). The web has Hardness 10 and 20 Hit Points. It has resistance 5 to bludgeoning and piercing and weakness 5 to slashing. At the end of the target's turn, if it has yet to Escape from enveloping web, the web grows, increasing the slowed value by 1, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 3}. The katroome can envelop up to three creatures at a time.

action->Glittering Scales.name: Glittering Scales
action->Glittering Scales.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect The katroome flaps its wings of prismatic scales. All creatures within a @Template[type:cone|distance:30] takes @Damage[9d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic] save. On a failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 minute, or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} on a critical failure.

Stair Spikes.name: Stair Spikes
Stair Spikes.descriptionHazard: A set of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} spikes stabs into the feet of anyone on the landing or front stairs.
Stair Spikes.disable: @Check[thievery|dc:22] (trained) to unhook the pressure plate or @Check[crafting|dc:24] (trained) to block the spikes

action->Look Sharp.name: Look Sharp
action->Look Sharp.description:
Trigger The door is opened while someone is standing on the stairs

Effect Spikes fire upward, dealing @Damage[(2d8+5)[piercing]] damage to all creatures standing on the landing or stairs (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic] save). On a failure, the creature takes a –5 status penalty to its Speed for 1 hour; on a critical failure it's also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1}.

Stumpfield War Chanter Choir.name: Stumpfield War Chanter Choir
Stumpfield War Chanter Choir.publicNotes: The goblin artists of Stumpfield love a good song, as most goblins do. While they aren't primarily warriors, these goblins have taken up their dogslicers to fight for the liberation of Isger. Their main advantage as a troop asset in skirmish encounters, though, is their ability to bolster their allies and flummox their enemies with their chants.

action->In Our Wake, Hell Will Break!.name: In Our Wake, Hell Will Break!
action->In Our Wake, Hell Will Break!.description: 30 feet. The war chanters constantly sing and cheer to spur their Hellbreaker allies. Each allied creature in the aura gains a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against mental effects.

action->Goblins Slice and Goblins Dice!.name: Goblins Slice and Goblins Dice!
action->Goblins Slice and Goblins Dice!.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The war chanters swing their dogslicers, attacking each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+2)[slashing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+8)[slashing]] damage

3 @Damage[(3d6+11)[slashing]] damage

action->Hark! The Goblins Chanters Sing!.name: Hark! The Goblins Chanters Sing!
action->Hark! The Goblins Chanters Sing!.description: The war chanters break out into a familiar song with unfamiliar rhyme. A number of enemies equal to the troop's current number of segments within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:25] save. Regardless of the outcome, a target becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 1 minute. On a failure, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round as they get distracted by the war chanters' rhymes (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} on a critical failure).

Stumpfield War Saboteurs.name: Stumpfield War Saboteurs
Stumpfield War Saboteurs.publicNotes: Stumpfield's artists enjoy the chaos of creation, which makes them well suited for the parallel chaos of destruction. Isn't sabotage just another form of art? Stumpfield certainly thinks so, and its denizens are happy to use their creative skills to waylay enemies and ambush parties.

action->Duck, Duck, Loose!.name: Duck, Duck, Loose!
action->Duck, Duck, Loose!.description:
Trigger The saboteurs are targeted by a Strike or an effect that requires a Reflex save

Effect The saboteurs scramble for the nearest cover. They become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} but gain @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.I9lfZUiCwMiGogVi]{Standard Cover} until the beginning of their next turn.

action->Rush and Steal.name: Rush and Steal
action->Rush and Steal.description: Quickly moving in with grasping hands, the Stumpfield war saboteurs take what they can. The saboteurs Stride up to twice their Speed. During this movement, the saboteurs Interact to pick up an unattended object no larger than 2 Bulk or attempt to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.RDXXE7wMrSPCLv5k]{Steal} an item from a creature they're adjacent to; the goblins can pick up or Steal as many objects as they have remaining segments in any combination.

action->Slapstick Traps.name: Slapstick Traps
action->Slapstick Traps.description:
Requirements The saboteurs' last action was to successfully Hide

Effect The saboteurs laid down falling obstacles as they move unseen. The saboteurs @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak} twice. Until the beginning of the saboteurs' next turn, any non-ally creature that moves into a square that the saboteurs snuck through takes @Damage[5d8[bludgeoning]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save). On a critical failure, the creature also falls @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. Once this trap has been triggered, the rest of the squares the saboteurs snuck through become difficult terrain until a creature spends 1 hour clearing it.

action->Slice and Dice!.name: Slice and Dice!
action->Slice and Dice!.description:
Frequency once per round;

Effect The saboteurs engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[1d8[slashing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+5)[slashing]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d8+9)[slashing]] damage

Swiftrun Clergy.name: Swiftrun Clergy
Swiftrun Clergy.publicNotes:
Swiftrun is a small settlement dedicated to Erastil, barely large enough to be considered a village. Its priests are devoted to community and peace, nature and nurture. And yet, sometimes the time comes when one must take up arms to protect their community, when words and gentleness aren't enough.

The Erastilian clergy is known more for their farming techniques than their magic, but their rural ways shouldn't fool anyone into thinking they can't defend themselves. Each priest is equipped with the divine power of Old Deadeye as well as sharpshooting skills conducive to Erastil's favored weapon, the longbow. These priests are prepared to defend their homes with their skills when they must, though they are never eager to shed blood and would rather spend their days tending their crops and community.

action->Longbow Volley.name: Longbow Volley
action->Longbow Volley.description: The Swiftrun clergy draw their longbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|traits:area-damage] save. When the regiment is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Swift Daggers.name: Swift Daggers
action->Swift Daggers.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect Swiftrun's clergy swiftly strike with their daggers against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d4+1)[piercing]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d4+7)[piercing]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d4+10)[piercing]] damage

action->Troop Spellcasting.name: Troop Spellcasting
action->Troop Spellcasting.description: When the Swiftrun clergy Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.

Thin Ice.name: Thin Ice
Thin Ice.descriptionHazard: The white-and-blue ice is less structurally sound than it looks.
Thin Ice.disable: @Check[nature|dc:31] or @Check[survival|dc:31] (expert) to spread out and prevent the ice from cracking further, or @Check[acrobatics|dc:31] or @Check[athletics|dc:31] (trained) to get off the ice safely as it cracks.
Thin Ice.reset: The river freezes over every night, restoring this hazard. The hazard disappears once the vernal equinox passes.

action->Crack.name: Crack
action->Crack.description:
Trigger Two or more creatures step onto the ice in area M8

Effect The ice beneath the party cracks. All creatures in area M8 must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30] save. On a failure, they fall into the freezing water, whereupon they must succeed at a basic @Check[fortitude|dc:30] save against @Damage[(4d10+22)[cold]] damage. On a critical failure, the creature must start holding their breath or risk drowning as their muscles lock and they begin to sink. A creature must spend an action to Swim to the shore and must then succeed at a @Check[athletics|dc:30] check to Climb up out of the water.

Umok Beastspeaker Circle.name: Umok Beastspeaker Circle
Umok Beastspeaker Circle.publicNotes:
The gnomes of Umok have a reticence to fire after the pyrrhic wildfire that razed the Chitterwood during the Goblinblood Wars. To help protect their home from unwanted arson, many gnomes of Umok have learned to communicate with animals, whose instincts can quickly sniff out unauthorized smoke or flame. The two groups work together to keep the town safe, with the animals serving as an early-warning system when threats approach.

Several Umok beastspeakers have joined the war effort, harnessing their primal magic and their connection with animals against the armies of Cheliax. Their powerful magics are used to summon beasts of the wild to defend Isger from forces that threaten the peace of their home, pitting primal power against fiends.

action->Animal Empathy.name: Animal Empathy
action->Animal Empathy.description: The beastspeaker circle can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals.

action->Muster Animals.name: Muster Animals
action->Muster Animals.description: The beastspeakers summon a group of wild animals to attack all creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet. The animals deal @Damage[3d8[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save). When the beastspeaker circle is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Staff Assault.name: Staff Assault
action->Staff Assault.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The beastspeakers grip their staves with two hands and attack all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic] save. The amount of damage depends on the number of actions used.

1 @Damage[(1d8+1)[bludgeoning]] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+6)[bludgeoning]] damage

3 @Damage[(2d8+10)[bludgeoning]] damage

action->Troop Spellcasting.name: Troop Spellcasting
action->Troop Spellcasting.description: When the beastspeaker circle Casts a Spell, the individual members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.

Varlario Demicci.name: Varlario Demicci
Varlario Demicci.blurb: Agender agent of the Whispering Way

weapon->Dagger.name: Dagger
weapon->Dagger.description: This small, bladed weapon is held in one hand and used to stab a creature in close combat. It can also be thrown.

weapon->Hand Crossbow.name: Hand Crossbow
weapon->Hand Crossbow.description: Sometimes referred to as an alley bow by rogues or ruffians, this small crossbow fires small bolts that are sometimes used to deliver poison to the target. It's small enough to be shot one-handed, but it still requires two hands to load.

armor->Explorer's Clothing.name: Explorer's Clothing
armor->Explorer's Clothing.description: Adventurers who don't wear armor travel in durable clothing. Though it's not armor and uses your unarmored defense proficiency, it still has a Dex Cap and can grant an item bonus to AC if etched with potency runes.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Shadow Blending.name: Shadow Blending
action->Shadow Blending.description:
Frequency once per minute

Trigger A creature attempts a flat check to target Varlario while they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} due to dim light or darkness

Effect Shadows wrap around Varlario's form. The DC of the flat check increases by 2.

action->Hidden Familiar.name: Hidden Familiar
action->Hidden Familiar.description: Varlario has @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} their bat familiar through the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.F1nlmqOIucch3Cmt]{Pet Cache} spell. They keep the bat contained except when they use shred spirit.

action->Shred Spirit.name: Shred Spirit
action->Shred Spirit.description:
Frequency once per minute

Effect Varlario sends forth their bat familiar as a phantasmal conduit to rip at the soul of a creature within 30 feet. The targeted creature must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:26|basic] save or take @Damage[9d6[spirit]] damage. Varlario then recovers Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt.

Velavi.name: Velavi
Velavi.publicNotes:
Knowledge is power, and no one is more keenly aware of this truth than velavis. These scribes and junior scholars are tasked with maintaining the unfathomably vast collections in Stygia's Academies of Lies. Velavis are the front faces of the library, greeting visitors and determining whether to grant their requests. Their organizational skills are among the best in Hell, and their memories are long, allowing them to easily navigate their home library with astonishing speed. Velavis provide another vital service for Hell: should another devil make a valuable discovery, they're expected to report to an Academy to have a velavi record it on a scroll or book the velavi crafts out of living flesh.

Each velavi is intimately familiar with the collections in their library, and they have unfettered access to other Academies' archives. Those seeking information in Hell might be tempted to seek a velavi's aid, but the velavi will demand a high price before conceding any information—even then, the flow of knowledge almost always goes one way, as velavis are tricky with what they share. Librarian devils are custodians, gatekeepers, and guardians; it's exceedingly rare for velavis to share their knowledge with anyone but a devil of higher rank, and even then, it must be bought or threatened out of them.
Velavi.allSavesBonus: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

armor->Scroll Robes.name: Scroll Robes
armor->Scroll Robes.description: Scroll robes are composed of paper alchemically treated for strength and flexibility. A layered structure prevents cutting and tearing, and for the purpose of calculating damage, the robes are considered to be cloth. The paper accepts all sorts of decoration, including magical writing, as detailed in the inscribed trait.

equipment->Writing Set.name: Writing Set
equipment->Writing Set.description: Using a writing set, you can draft correspondence and scribe scrolls. A set includes stationery, including a variety of paper and parchment, as well as ink, a quill or inkpen, sealing wax, and a simple seal. If you've written a large amount, you can refill your kit with extra ink and paper.

consumable->Scroll of Phantasmal Minion.name: Scroll of Phantasmal Minion (Rank 1) (Scroll Robes)
consumable->Scroll of Phantasmal Minion.description:
@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.xqmHD8JIjak15lRk]{Phantasmal Minion}

Casting a Spell from a scroll requires holding the scroll in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell.

The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell also gains the appropriate trait for your tradition (arcane, divine, occult, or primal).

Any physical costs are provided when a scroll is created, so you don't need to provide them when casting from a scroll. If the spell requires a locus, you must have that locus to Cast the Spell from a scroll.

Note: To create a scroll or wand of a specific spell, drag the spell from the compendium or compendium browser into the inventory of a PC, NPC, or loot actor.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Frightening Shush.name: Frightening Shush
action->Frightening Shush.description:
The velavi makes an intimidating shushing motion. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a @Check[will|dc:18] save. The velavi can't use Frightening Shush again for [[/br 1d4 #Recharge Frightening Shush]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}. While a creature is frightened by this ability, it's @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to the velavi.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature also can't make any sounds while it's frightened, as if under the effect of the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.gIdDLrbswTV3OBJy]{Silence} spell.

weapon->Crossbow.name: Crossbow
weapon->Crossbow.description: This ranged weapon has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame called a tiller. The tiller has a mechanism to lock the bowstring in place, attached to a trigger mechanism that releases the tension and launches a bolt.

weapon->Longsword.name: Longsword
weapon->Longsword.description: Longswords, also known as arming swords, can be one-edged or two-edged swords. Their blades are heavy and they're between 3 and 4 feet in length.

shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).name: Sturdy Shield (Minor)
shield->Sturdy Shield (Minor).description:
With a superior design and excellent craftsmanship, this steel shield has higher Hardness than its non-magical counterparts, making it harder to break and destroy.

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 64, and BT 32.

armor->Hellknight Plate.name: Hellknight Plate
armor->Hellknight Plate.description:
Hellknights wear a variety of armors decorated with designs specific to the order. Hellknight half plate is the armor of choice for Hellknight signifiers, and Hellknight breastplate serves those in the order who lack the training to wear heavy armor.

A character who is a member of the Hellknights has access to these uncommon armors.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->+3 to Reflex Saves vs. Area Effe.name: +3 to Reflex Saves vs. Area Effects

action->Punishing Cut.name: Punishing Cut
action->Punishing Cut.description: Viro makes a longsword Strike. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional die of weapon damage and 1d4 persistent bleed damage.

action->Shielded Charge.name: Shielded Charge
action->Shielded Charge.description:
Requirements Viro is wielding a shield

Effect Viro Raises his Shield and then Strides twice. If he ends his movement adjacent to a foe, that foe is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to Viro's Strikes until the end of Viro's current turn.

Warshard Wight.name: Warshard Wight
Warshard Wight.publicNotes:
Wights are intelligent undead spawned through inescapable cycles of spite. This spite might come from their own malevolent will in life, or can be instilled by necromantic rituals, typically involving the desecration of burial sites. Wights usually haunt burial grounds, catacombs, or other places of the dead. Their hunger is targeted toward the living—those whose pumping hearts and ruddy warmth inspire visceral hatred.

As many types of wights exist as types of people from which they might be created. Hulking brutes, skittering sneaks, and cunning tinkers all make for different wights with different niches to fill. The environment, too, plays a part in determining a wight's special abilities and defenses. Frost wights, for instance, can be found in parts of the world where exposure is a common end and the resentment of being left alone in the wild is a common source of spite. Durable and sustained by void energy, wights can last in harsh environments without decaying the way some lesser undead do.

A single wight can wreak significant havoc if it is compelled to rise from its tomb. Because creatures slain under a wight's curse can become wights as well, all it takes is a single wight and a handful of unlucky graveyard visitors to create a veritable horde of these undead. Thus, canny priests and adventurers know that the best solution to a wight problem is swift and total eradication. Care must be taken, though, to destroy wight spawn before attempting to destroy the parent wight, for spawn without a master gain the ability to create spawn of their own.

action->Urgathoa's Weapon.name: Urgathoa's Weapon
action->Urgathoa's Weapon.description: The warshard wight has a scythe arm resembling a Child of Urgathoa.

action->Final Spite.name: Final Spite
action->Final Spite.description:
Trigger The warshward wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points

Effect The warshard wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. It doesn't gain any temporary Hit Points from drain life on this Strike.

action->Bloody Wail.name: Bloody Wail
action->Bloody Wail.description:
The warshard wight cries out in an unearthly shriek. Living creatures within 50 feet must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:23] save. The warshard wight can't make another bloody wail for [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Success The creature bleeds from their ears and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 round.

Failure The creature bleeds from their ears and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 round.

Critical Failure As failure, and the creature takes a –2 status penalty to saving throws against drain life.

action->Drain Life.name: Drain Life
action->Drain Life.description: When the warshard wight damages a living creature with a melee Strike using an unarmed attack or its bound weapon, the warshard wight gains 5 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:20] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}. Further damage dealt by the warshard wight increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4}.

action->Warshard Wight Spawn.name: Warshard Wight Spawn
action->Warshard Wight Spawn.description: A living humanoid slain by a warshard wight's scything blade or claw Strike rises as a spawned wight after [[/br 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}. This spawned wight is under the command of the warshard wight that killed it. It doesn't have drain life or warshard wight spawn and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for as long as it's a spawned wight. If its creator dies, the spawned wight becomes a full-fledged, autonomous warshard wight; it regains its free will, gains drain life and warshard wight spawn, and is no longer clumsy.

Zombie Giant Bat.name: Zombie Giant Bat
Zombie Giant Bat.publicNotes:
While big bats are certainly not uncommon in dark caves and abandoned ruins and may instill fear in squeamish spelunkers, the so-called giant bat is a true monster, weighing well over 100 pounds and having a wingspan of nearly 15 feet. It primarily eats fruit and bugs, but can be incited to violence through fear or hunger. Giant bat attacks can quickly give rise to rumors of more dangerous monsters—many mistake these massive animals for some sort of demon or vampiric monster. But like other bats, giant bats are simply social and intelligent mammals. They are sometimes used as mounts by smaller humanoids, commonly those who hail from or dwell in mountainous or underground regions.

A wide range of bats dwell throughout the world. Most of these nocturnal animals are harmless insectivores, but deadly breeds of vampire bats and oversized bats the size of horses pose much more significant threats to adventurers.

action->Slow.name: Slow
action->Slow.description: A zombie is permanently @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} and can't use reactions.

action->Clumsy Flier.name: Clumsy Flier
action->Clumsy Flier.description: While a zombie giant bat can fly, its wings are barely able to keep it aloft. If it's airborne when it takes a critical hit or any amount of slashing damage, it falls and it can't Fly on its next turn.

action->Rotting Aura.name: Rotting Aura
action->Rotting Aura.description: 10 feet. The zombie giant bat emits an aura of rot and disease that cause wounds to fester. Any living creature that starts its turn within the aura and isn't at full Hit Points takes @Damage[1d6[void]] damage.
